title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
reviewText: Got this stove for it's simmer capability. A bit pricey and has plastic parts. Seems to work alright with Coleman fuel.  I did not try the multifuel capability. It is still too big to nest in cook kit.  I prefer the older Canadian Coleman that is no longer made.

reviewText:
I was torn between this stove and the MSR Whisperlite international.  I went with this one because it was cheaper, others recommended it and it seemed to have a better design than the other stove with less parts to get gunked up with dirt or grime or whatever else you run into while backpacking.  I like how stable the unit is.  There is this nifty elevated ring you can see in the picture.  You can move it around to level the legs better when you have it sitting on a rock or whatever.  It does have a stupid "hi/lo" sticker instead of painted or engraved markings but if you can't figure where high and where low are after using it once then should you really be out backpacking?

My first use went flawlessly.  It burned with large flames for the first 20 seconds until it was primed then it calmed down.  It burns with a very wide, blue and hot flame even on low.  This will prevent common issue with scorching as seen in the pocket rocket stoves.  The adjustment knob is located in a silly place right under the burner bowl.  As long as you aren't completely inept it should not be a problem though.  The fuel tank did get hot to the touch when on "high" which made me a bit nervous but one rarely cooks on high and the cool mountain air should keep it cool enough.

I fashioned a nice little pack box from the cardboard it came in using masking and packaging tape.  It doesn't fully enclose the stove but it will protect the burner and the valve/knobs from getting broken or bent while in your pack.

4/5 stars because its a little chunky but I expect it to perform better than any other stove.
text: TITLE: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove DESCRIPTION: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory. REVIEW: I was torn between this stove and the MSR Whisperlite international. I went with this one because it was cheaper, others recommended it and it seemed to have a better design than the other stove with less parts to get gunked up with dirt or grime or whatever else you run into while backpacking. I like how stable the unit is. There is this nifty elevated ring you can see in the picture. You can move it around to level the legs better when you have it sitting on a rock or whatever. It does have a stupid "hi/lo" sticker instead of painted or engraved markings but if you can't figure where high and where low are after using it once then should you really be out backpacking? My first use went flawlessly. It burned with large flames for the first 20 seconds until it was primed then it calmed down. It burns with a very wide, blue and hot flame even on low. This will prevent common issue with scorching as seen in the pocket rocket stoves. The adjustment knob is located in a silly place right under the burner bowl. As long as you aren't completely inept it should not be a problem though. The fuel tank did get hot to the touch when on "high" which made me a bit nervous but one rarely cooks on high and the cool mountain air should keep it cool enough. I fashioned a nice little pack box from the cardboard it came in using masking and packaging tape. It doesn't fully enclose the stove but it will protect the burner and the valve/knobs from getting broken or bent while in your pack. 4/5 stars because its a little chunky but I expect it to perform better than any other stove.

title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove

reviewText: this is the 3rd coleman stove I own. the first 2 where the cheapest model available from walmart and since they would not work for more than 4 or 5 camping escapades I decided to give this better quality version a try. Big mistake. I took it for a 4 day camping spring trip into the smokey mountains and used 100% unleaded gas (no ethanol added) and it worked beautifully the first 2 days. on the third it was already showing signs of poor combustion, it was hard to start, and would only build up the pressure and temperature needed for it to work on it's own, after 15 minutes of pumping! (in hot weather!)... I recommend you either go with propane if you're going to use it every now and then, or go with the real deal and spend a couple of hundred dollars on the most expensive ones (I hear MSR for $200-$300 is a good brand but never tried it, I'm personally going to get a britelyt stove for $100 because I have one of their lanterns and it is a superb quality product) I'm not giving it 1 star just because if I had the patience to open and clean the thing every other day of use and perhaps if I used the FAR MORE EXPENSIVE coleman gas, it would work fine, but as for me I'm returning it and I'm done with coleman liquid fuel powered products (also had 3 lanterns before buying the brytelit AKA petromax lantern).

title: Coleman Cooler Standard Drain Plug Assembly - 1" Shaft Length
reviewText: So if you're replacing your cooler drain assembly, this definitely fits.  The problem is that if your replacing the drain and the original drain assembly has a large nut on the interior to back up the gasket, this isn't the same.  Yes, it fits, but it's difficult to tighten in order to snug up the new gasket.  I haven't pressed this repair into service yet, so the jury is still out.  If you have a large hex key, you can tighten this assembly properly by placing the hex key on the interior fitting.  It's hollow (as it should be) but it is a large hex opening.  I don't know about you, but I've got all the tools I thought I would ever need, and I just don't have that large a hex key.  As far as I can tell, if you have a large enough SAE sized hex key you should be able to get this good and snug enough to compress the interior rubber washer.  I just didn't have one large enough.  I guess I just don't have enough tools.  Well, I'm off to buy more tools.  I hope this review was helpful.

If you have a large hex key, you can tighten this assembly properly by placing the hex key on the interior fitting.  It's hollow (as it should be) but it is a large hex opening.  I don't know about you, but I've got all the tools I thought I would ever need, and I just don't have that large a hex key.  As far as I can tell, if you have a large enough SAE sized hex key you should be able to get this good and snug enough to compress the interior rubber washer.  I just didn't have one large enough.  I guess I just don't have enough tools.  Well, I'm off to buy more tools.  I hope this review was helpful.

title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. reviewText:
First...the Coleman Roadtrip grill and this bag are VERY HEAVY. That's not a bad thing but it was MUCH MUCH heavier than I imagined it would be. I've got a el-cheapo gas grill that's much larger that weighs HALF what this portable one does. NOT impressed with the weight to features.

This is a nice THICK bag. The rollers are a nice add-on to drag it around by.
This is a very big bag. I easily fit inside it and I'm not a small guy (don't ask WHY I tried).
The pockets for the burners is pretty tight and the velcro is aggravating the way it's orientated.
Well built...does the job but, again, not what I would term 'portable'. I would NOT go this route again.

title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: I would say I love this except for the color and the weight of the outer fabric. It looks like something I've "borrowed" from the military - there is no reason camping can't be colorful and fun. We had nights where the temperature fell to 24 deg F with pouring rain. Even though the sleeping bag got wet due to a leaky tent I remained warm and toasty all night.

title: Coleman Road Trip Cast-Iron Griddle
description:
Coleman Roadtrip Cast Iron Accessory Griddle sits on top of a propane burner for easy cooking! Expand your camp fare menu with this Cast Iron Accessory Griddle from Coleman! Perfect for pancakes, scrambled eggs and MUCH more! Made with cast iron for even heat distribution on the entire surface. Measures 12 x 12". Get yours, right now! Coleman Roadtrip Cast Iron Accessory Griddle Turn your Coleman Road Trip grill or campfire stove grate into a portable diner with this Coleman cast-iron griddle. Made of heavy-duty, porcelain-coated cast iron, the griddle is easy to clean and long lasting. More importantly, the griddle opens up a world of menu options for your camping trip, including pancakes, fried eggs, bacon, omelets, grilled cheese sandwiches, and more. The griddle--which is compatible with Coleman 9941 and 9949 Road Trip grills--measures approximately 12 by 12 inches and weighs 7 pounds. The griddle is not compatible with Coleman's 9928 Road Trip Pro Series grills. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: We will be having pancakes at our next tailgate this is well built and fits great the price can't be beat

title: Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag
description:
Sleep comfortably--even when the temperature dips below freezing--with the Coleman Big Game Big and Tall Adult Sleeping Bag. Long enough for people up to 6 feet 5 inches in height, this adult sleeping bag is designed to keep you warm in temperatures as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit. The cotton canvas cover and cozy cotton flannel liner retain heat, so you feel warm from head to toe. For additional comfort, a Thermolock draft tube helps prevent heat from escaping through the zipper, and a ComfortCuff surrounds your face in soft, warm fabric. Fiberlock construction keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A two-way patented ZipPlow zipper system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. And the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N Roll integrated packing system make this backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. The Big Game Sleeping Bag is machine washable and comes with a matching flannel pillow. Stay snugly warm as you slumber on camping and hunting excursions with the Coleman Big Game sleeping bag, which is rated to temperatures as low as -5 degrees Fahrenheit. It's filled with five pounds of Holofill 808 insulation, which offers high-loft, hollow-core fibers that prevent body heat from escaping, thus holding more air inside the bag for good warmth. This super king-sized, rectangular sleeping bag measures 40 by 85 inches, and will fit most sleepers up to 6 foot, 5 inches. Other features include a heavy duty cotton cover material, cotton flannel liner, an included pillow, and Coleman's exclusive set of ComfortSmart sleeping bag technologies, including:
  • ZipPlow: Prevents zipper snags by plowing fabric away from zipper
  • FiberLock: Prevents insulation from shifting, increasing durability
  • ThermoLock: Reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer
  • Zipper GlideTailoring: Allows smooth zipper operation around the corner
  • Comfort Cuff: Surrounds your face with softness
  • Roll Control: Locks bag in place for easier rolling
About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: Buyer beware !  Once you zip Coleman sleeping bags together, they can't be separated.  I talked directly to Coleman customer service.  Even they don't know how to get them apart.

title: Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Grill Case for LX Series Grills
description: Add even more portability to your Coleman RoadTrip LX Series Grill when you pack it inside a Coleman RoadTrip Rolling Case. This heavy-duty, weather-resistant case includes easy-glide wheels for more mobility and top and side handles for better control. A durable zipper and inner straps secure the grill the entire trip, and inside pockets fit accessories and condiments for whatever meal youre planning to cook up.
reviewText:
I chose this because I thought it would be convenient and quality.  I liked the idea of a case that would carry the grill and all the accessories - grill grates, tank adapter, etc.  Unfortunately, the quality is lacking and there's not a good way to stow the handle as far as I can tell.

The very first time I went to put the grill into the case, I noticed first it wouldn't fit.  Ok, so you have to remove the handle - no big deal right?  But there's no place to put the handle - it doesn't fit will in the external mesh pocket, which is better suited for the 20 lb tank adaper hose.  Not a huge deal and I could deal with that.

Unfortunately when I went to strap in the grill inside the case using the supplied straps, one of them broke.  I was lightly cinching down the lower velcro strap and it simply pulled out - came unstitched from the case, rendering it useless.  Not what I expected from what should be a heavy duty case.  Not worth the effort of sending it back, but this shouldn't happen.

title: Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler
description: The Coleman 52 qt. Xtreme Cooler is perfect for weekend getaways, with the technology to keep ice up to five days in 90-degree weather and the capacity to hold 82 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Durable plastic construction. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 82 cans. Includes hinged lid with 4 cup holders and 2-way handle. Dimensions: 27.75L x 14.95W x 17.25H in..
reviewText:
I just got back from a six day camping trip. I filled this cooler  with 20 pounds of ice cubes (leaving about 1/3rd of the space free for food) and much to my surprise, the ice was gone early today but the water was icy cold still and the remaining food was fine.

 In other words, this cooler performs phenomenally if the ratio of ice to food is what is needed.

 I did keep it in the shade, but the temperatures were in the 80s, and I am very very happy with it's excellent performance.

title: Coleman 70 Quart Xtreme 5 Cooler
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time, and make the fun last longer, when you bring a Coleman 70 Quart Xtreme 5 Cooler. Xtreme technology uses an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls to keep your items cold for up to 5 days. Large enough to hold 98 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for a whole group of people. Whether you're headed on a long weekend camping trip, a roadtrip, or out to a BBQ, the 2-way handles make getting to your destination easy. Once you arrive, the cup holders molded into the lid will keep your cold drink close while you enjoy the day. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain makes it easy to empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so you're ready for your next fun adventure. The Coleman 50-Quart Xtreme Cooler is the ideal traveling companion. The large icy blue cooler and white lid are lined with a full two full inches of insulation and the hinged lid also features a secure and tight gasket seal, ensuring that your cooler will keep food and beverages chilled for up to five days at temperatures of up to 90 degrees. Holding up to 98 beverage cans with ice, this cooler features two-way handles for easy lift and maneuvering in tight spaces, and the sump-lined drain promises easy clean-up and no-tilt draining--a plus for this large capacity cooler. This models lid can be fitted with 24-inch cords (not included) for additional dry storage--lawn chairs, beach towels, picnic blankets, etc.--on top. The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText:
It's a typical coleman cooler.  Plastic and and works good.  I've owned a few and they work great.  I put two bags of ice and lots of drinks in the cooler the day we got it.  5 days later there was still some ice.  Have not had it out camping yet and that will be the real test.  I'll update then.

UPDATE:  Second campout and it leaks and the handel came off.  JUNK!  JUNK!  JUNK!

Not worth $10 in my opinion.

title: Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels, Blue
description:
Food, drinksand the funlast longer, when you roll in with the Coleman 100-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. To be exact, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 160 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks so the entire party remains fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. Wherever youre headedcampgrounds, tailgate parking lot, or across a ball fieldthe heavy-duty wheels and large tow handle let you roll over almost any terrain with little effort. The additional 2-way side handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drink close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler, so youre ready for your next fun adventure in no time. Carry up to 160 cans with ease--and store ice for up to five days even when it's 90 degrees outside--in the Xtreme 5 100-Quart Wheeled Cooler by Coleman. With two large wheels, two-way handles and a comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation, this cooler is easy to roll even on rough terrain. After the party, it's easy to drain thanks to a rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt draining. Features:
  • Keeps ice up to 5 days at 90 degrees Fahrenheit
  • Holds 160 cans
  • Hinged lid with beverage holders
  • Two-way handles for flexible easy carrying
  • Comfortable tow handle for one-hand operation
  • Rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain for no-tilt easy draining
  • Two large wheels for easy pulling, even on rough terrain
  • Made with Thermozone insulation that contains no toxic CFCs, HFCs, or HCFCs
Specifications:
  • Exterior Dimensions: 36.5 by 16.75 by 18.25 inches
  • Interior Dimensions: 23.75 by 11.75 by 13 inches
  • Weight Empty: 18 pounds
  • Made in the USA
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: First new cooler leaked from the drain spout.  Second replacement cooler - leaked from the drain spout.  The first one leaked around the gasket.  The second one leaked around the cap - which is not tight enough or fitted properly. Two new ones, both defective - you decide if you want to buy this product

title: Coleman Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler
description: The Coleman 62 qt. Xtreme Cooler features Xtreme 5 technology and the capacity to hold up to 85 cans. It features two-way handles for easy lifting and carrying, and the rustproof, leak-resistant channel drain allows for no-tilt draining. Dimensions: 26L x 14.75W x 14H inches. About Coleman Company Inc. Fueled by poor eyesight and blind ambition, W.C. Coleman began his company with the invention of his gas lamps in 1905. Twenty years later, the company saw a country at war and an Army without adequate stoves. The Coleman pocket stove is today considered one of the two most important innovations in non-combat equipment in the war effort. It is this commitment to quality and innovation that continues to push the Coleman brand to create essential and reliable products that are convenient and easy to use. High-quality plastic construction with hinged lid. Features Xtreme 5 technology and Thermozone insulation. Designed to hold up to 85 cans. Comes with tow handle, 2-way handles, and sturdy wheels.
reviewText: We have much higher expectations for this cooler in regards to holding ice for a longer number of days.  It is not coming through.

title: Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case
description: Protect your lantern with the Coleman Propane Lantern Carry Case so it'll be ready to go the next time you head outdoors. This tough, double-walled case is made of polyethylene housing. The base has room for extra mantles and miscellaneous other items. The case latch locks securely and is positioned at the top for easy loading and unloading. Simply let your lantern cool and place your lantern inside the case, and you're ready to head on to your next adventure.
reviewText: I have a Coleman 5155 and this case fits the lantern perfectly.  It protects the glass globe when it's in the bed of the truck with the rest of the camping gear.

Exactly as described. Fit my 40 year old lantern like a glove. Can finally get rid of the original cardboard box.

title: Coleman Camper’s Toilet Paper, 3 Rolls
description: Take an everyday hygiene necessity on camping trips with Coleman Campers Toilet Paper. Includes 3 rolls of biodegradable, septic-safe 2-ply toilet paper that easily fits in any camping backpack. Each roll contains at least 65 squares of premium tissue and comes in a case that also doubles as a dispenser for added convenience. Compact and convenient, Coleman Campers Toilet Paper lets you take an essential indoor comfort wherever the trail leads.
reviewText:
The pictures, description, etc all clearly state/show that this product comes as three individually packaged rolls in a bag. This was important to me, as I'd planned to split them up between three bags to be kept in my cars. However, the individual rolls are NOT in individual cases/bags, and in fact are just loose in the primary bag.

Yes, they're useful as toilet paper, but only water resistant and durable if kept together in the original packaging. Not what I wanted.

title: Coleman Camper’s Toilet Paper, 3 Rolls
description: Take an everyday hygiene necessity on camping trips with Coleman Campers Toilet Paper. Includes 3 rolls of biodegradable, septic-safe 2-ply toilet paper that easily fits in any camping backpack. Each roll contains at least 65 squares of premium tissue and comes in a case that also doubles as a dispenser for added convenience. Compact and convenient, Coleman Campers Toilet Paper lets you take an essential indoor comfort wherever the trail leads.
reviewText:
Toilet paper in the woods is for sissys.

Here's what you do... squat and do your biz... wait for a squirrel to come by.  Grab said squirrel and use as anal brush. Sometimes after too many smore's the night before I have to two-squirrel it.  No biggie.

 It's quick, clean and leaves no trace (except of course on the squirrel - but it will rain soon).  I mean who needs toilet paper anyways when you have tree rats?

Right?

Orrrr... just steal a roll of that Charmin stuff from your mom's house and bend it so the core closes, place in zip lock bag (also from mom's house) and stick in pack.  Or napkins from McDonalds.  That all works too.

Or you could go blow $7.33 on this little gem of an idea.

title: Coleman Cast Iron Skillet
description: Grill up burgers, steaks and chicken on your next outdoor adventure with a Coleman Cast Iron Skillet. You'll have plenty of room to cook on the easy-to-clean, non-stick, 10-in,-diameter cooking surface. Raised ribs ensure an even temperature throughout, and the pour spouts makes for easy draining when you're done. A hole in the molded handle makes storing at home or at the campsite simple.
reviewText: Over priced considering you have to order regulator and hose separately. Cheaply made in China, instructions very poor. The burner holes were mostly plugged with paint and had to use a drill bit to clean out. Flame adjustment not the greatest, if you turn it down anywhere below high the flame goes out. Will keep it as it will work for what I use it for, sand shading veneer for inlay projects.

title: Coleman Butane / Propane Mix Fuel (Mix Fuel 7.75 oz.) - 7.75 oz.
description: Butane/propane mix canisters are lightweight, resalable, and easily connect to stoves and lanterns. Most canister appliances are lightweight and simple to use, so if you are a backpacker who counts ounces and appreciates convenience this fuel is for you. Downsides are that canisters can't be recycled, and in subfreezing temperatures, the fuel does not perform well. Or at all. Cold temperatures affect the pressure in the canister, so performance is best in mild to moderate conditions, main advantages: convenience and light weight includes a collar notch on top of the canister that provides easy alignment in appliances
reviewText: I rated the fuel canister three stars because it's heavy and large. I placed it inside my cook kit,fits nicely. For those on long backpacking treks(i.e. Appalachian Trail)they may want this size,but not for the minimalist. It takes too much space in my daypack and bug out bag and 75 cubic inch pack.,too much weight as well. Best left for car camping for a back up to the Coleman two or one burner propane stove.

title: Coleman 150 qt. Cooler
description: Built to serve the largest of groups, the Thermozone-insulated Coleman 150 qt. Cooler has room for up to 233 cans. It features a hinged lid with four cup holders and two-way handles. The hinged lid closes securely and allows for easy one-hand opening.   You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. High-quality plastic construction in blue. Thermozone insulated cooler holds up to 233 cans. Hinged lid contains 4 molded beverage holders. Features 2-way handles for easy carrying..
reviewText: I got this chest primarily as extra capacity to haul buffalo meat from South Dakota to Texas.  It's a supplement to a more expensive chest I already had. (There's lots of meat on a buffalo).  It kept the meat cold and mostly frozen for the trip back to Texas, not too difficult since much of the time the temperature was well below freezing for most of the trip.  There are no latches.  The friction fit of the lid keeps it closed.  It is hard to open unless there is a lot of weight in it.  With weight in it, I go to the back and lift up -  not bad at all.  When empty or near empty there is nothing to conveniently hold the bottom down while pulling the top open, - hence the 3 rating.  I do have this chest in a 70 qt and it's ok and does a good job of holding ice.  Since it's smaller, it's much easier to lift the lid but this 150 is really difficult to open.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
Very large and roomy.  Took two adults and 4 kids, lots of extra room for bags and toys.  The room dividers were ok, nothing more than a thin sheet but my 8 and 9 yr old loved it.

Setup is a two person job, I did put it up once but it is hard to do alone.  No issues with product, great tent and its big!  windows and door were solid as normal, this is the second Colman tent and would never buy anything else.  The floor is tarp like and it always keeps the rain and moisture out.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
I received this tent about a week ago knowing full well it would be used right away. Though it was an eight person tent, I was the only one sleeping in it but I needed the extra room for my cot and all of my gear. The time of year is mid summer so I thought this tent would be perfect.
When the tent came, I opened the item right away to take inventory and to do a test assembly in the field across from my home. Please understand I do not do have a lot of current camping experience but I have had experience over the years and thought it should be pretty straight forward. The tent includes the tent, rainfly, stakes and poles all enclosed with instructions in a zippered bag. The tent instructions say that it would be better if a second person was available to help erect the tent. There was no second person included so I did it solo. It takes some patience but it can be done. Don't get ahead of yourself, follow the steps and complete them one at a time and you'll have success even if you are solo. The hardest part is the initial raising and placing of the main support poles. If you first secure the main poles (one at a time) on the side opposite the door, you'll find it a bit easier as you can stand inside the door (if it is zipped open,) allowing you to be closer to the center of the roof as it is raised. After that, everything is a bit easier. Side poles come next and then clip and secure everything.
 You may find that the zippers on the front door are under a bit of tension. I found that if I used the top zipper to close half the door and the lower zipper to close the lower half, it opened and closed much easier than if you used one zipper to do the whole door. Also, you'll find if you leave the internal door window unzipped (open), the door zippers work easier. One of the back window zippers kept loosing it's lanyard as the metal tang on the zipper wedge seemed a bit too open to keep the cord in place so I simple took my uni-tool pliers and gave it a gentle squeeze closing the loop and thus holding the lanyard more securely. No failure on the zippers thus far.
 The floor is a Tyvec type material and a tub type design. It seemed fairly rugged but I stilled used a tarp ground cloth as a good foundation. My cot has 6 legs and in four days never left as much as even an impression on the floor. I did have a thorn or two pierce the floor while camping, my fault for not making sure the ground was completely clear. But it did serve as a reminder that even though it may look rugged, you can still easily put a hole in it.
 On my camping trip, I used what I had learned to erect the tent and repeated the process with out too much difficulty even on my own. It is important to note at this point that I did not have success putting on the rainfly during my practice run as the wind was too great. So, if you can, save the rain fly for when there is little or no wind, especially if you are by yourself. I slept in the tent for three days without the rain fly and it was great. The huge screen panels allow great views of the night sky and give more than ample ventilation.
 The forecast for the fourth night was for rain so I installed the rainfly. This is where someone may have dropped the ball in writing the instructions. First, the instructions call for placing the Arbor  rods through the specified points prior to draping the fly over the tent. If you have two people, perhaps its possible. But, it was much easier to drape the fly over the tent and THEN install the arbor rods. Second, the instructions show four bungee securing loops, one at each corner of the fly when in fact there are 8 bungee loops with hooks. The hooks are then stretched and secured to the stake loops at the four main corners of the tent. This design could use a little refining as the steel tent stake anchor loops are pressed firmly to the ground under pressure from both the tent stakes themselves and the four tent support rods. Trying to get the bungee hook attached to the tent stake loop as directed may require you to do a little excavation with your fingernails in order to access enough surface area of the loop to secure the hook. Also, these bungees are under pressure so when you are stretching them, be careful one doesnt slip and hit you right in the face...like it did me.
 Once the fly was up, I was ready for the rain. Or was I? In total, we had about an inch and a half of rain that fell over a 24 hour period. In that time, you could see a slow deterioration of the rain proof capability in both the rain fly and the tent. At first it looked like it was coming in around the tent stake anchor points (it felt wet in these areas), then I noticed that the fly itself started to seep through the fabric in several areas. Later still, I noticed that the tent fabric areas that were not protected/covered by the rain fly became saturated and accumulating moisture seeped through. I used several t shirts and socks to soak up the water. In total, I estimate there was at least a quart or more that leaked through. Fortunately, the ground cloth raised the floor of the tent slightly and accumulating water stayed to the outer perimeter of the floor. Considering it was a new tent, I am pretty disappointed at the lack of water proofing that was done to both the tent and the rain fly. I expected some moisture, but not to the degree that I experienced.
 As far as rain is concerned, there is a another design feature that could be improved and that is the rain fly arbor over the front door. With out any wind, rain can still wet the door. And, since the door is at an angle, when you unzip the door, the door swings inside and all of the rain on the outside of the door is now on your floor. You can minimize this by tapping on the door before you open it and most of the rain water will fall to the outside, but not all of it. An over hang of another 12-18 inches would take care of this.
 One final note. The tent take down was pretty simple all but drying it out before I could roll everything up which takes time. Try as I may, I could not roll up everything tight enough to be able to fit everything back into the bag that it came in. A bigger bag might be nice. I have since purchased a can of water proofing spray and will treat everything and give it another go. If I hadn't received such a huge discount when I purchased it, I would  probably return the tent because of the leaking.

One of the back window zippers kept loosing it's lanyard as the metal tang on the zipper wedge seemed a bit too open to keep the cord in place so I simple took my uni-tool pliers and gave it a gentle squeeze closing the loop and thus holding the lanyard more securely. No failure on the zippers thus far.
 The floor is a Tyvec type material and a tub type design. It seemed fairly rugged but I stilled used a tarp ground cloth as a good foundation. My cot has 6 legs and in four days never left as much as even an impression on the floor. I did have a thorn or two pierce the floor while camping, my fault for not making sure the ground was completely clear. But it did serve as a reminder that even though it may look rugged, you can still easily put a hole in it.
 On my camping trip, I used what I had learned to erect the tent and repeated the process with out too much difficulty even on my own. It is important to note at this point that I did not have success putting on the rainfly during my practice run as the wind was too great. So, if you can, save the rain fly for when there is little or no wind, especially if you are by yourself. I slept in the tent for three days without the rain fly and it was great. The huge screen panels allow great views of the night sky and give more than ample ventilation.
 The forecast for the fourth night was for rain so I installed the rainfly. This is where someone may have dropped the ball in writing the instructions. First, the instructions call for placing the Arbor  rods through the specified points prior to draping the fly over the tent. If you have two people, perhaps its possible. But, it was much easier to drape the fly over the tent and THEN install the arbor rods. Second, the instructions show four bungee securing loops, one at each corner of the fly when in fact there are 8 bungee loops with hooks. The hooks are then stretched and secured to the stake loops at the four main corners of the tent. This design could use a little refining as the steel tent stake anchor loops are pressed firmly to the ground under pressure from both the tent stakes themselves and the four tent support rods. Trying to get the bungee hook attached to the tent stake loop as directed may require you to do a little excavation with your fingernails in order to access enough surface area of the loop to secure the hook. Also, these bungees are under pressure so when you are stretching them, be careful one doesnt slip and hit you right in the face...like it did me.
 Once the fly was up, I was ready for the rain. Or was I? In total, we had about an inch and a half of rain that fell over a 24 hour period. In that time, you could see a slow deterioration of the rain proof capability in both the rain fly and the tent. At first it looked like it was coming in around the tent stake anchor points (it felt wet in these areas), then I noticed that the fly itself started to seep through the fabric in several areas. Later still, I noticed that the tent fabric areas that were not protected/covered by the rain fly became saturated and accumulating moisture seeped through. I used several t shirts and socks to soak up the water. In total, I estimate there was at least a quart or more that leaked through. Fortunately, the ground cloth raised the floor of the tent slightly and accumulating water stayed to the outer perimeter of the floor. Considering it was a new tent, I am pretty disappointed at the lack of water proofing that was done to both the tent and the rain fly. I expected some moisture, but not to the degree that I experienced.
 As far as rain is concerned, there is a another design feature that could be improved and that is the rain fly arbor over the front door. With out any wind, rain can still wet the door. And, since the door is at an angle, when you unzip the door, the door swings inside and all of the rain on the outside of the door is now on your floor. You can minimize this by tapping on the door before you open it and most of the rain water will fall to the outside, but not all of it. An over hang of another 12-18 inches would take care of this.
 One final note. The tent take down was pretty simple all but drying it out before I could roll everything up which takes time. Try as I may, I could not roll up everything tight enough to be able to fit everything back into the bag that it came in. A bigger bag might be nice. I have since purchased a can of water proofing spray and will treat everything and give it another go. If I hadn't received such a huge discount when I purchased it, I would  probably return the tent because of the leaking.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText: We were a little hesitant about this buy, but can't be happier. It's a great size for two adults and two kids, everyone can stand up in it, move around without bumping into anyone else, etc. The privacy flaps are tie-down, but if the kids want their own "room" or "rooms" we can make that happen, which is nice. This is definitely a summer to mid-fall tent; the last time we used it was early to mid-September (maybe around the high 40s or low 50s at night?), and that was as cold as I'd be willing to use it. But in terms of summer, the air flow of this tent is GREAT, and better than any other tent I've used. Setup is a little tricky the first time, but after that is easy to remember. Repacking the tent into its original bag is a challenge, though, which may have to be remedied with a larger stuff sack.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText: For about $100 dollars great value. We are a family of four with two small children. We bought this tent to have enough space in the event of a rain day. I was disappointed after the first rain, of pooling along the inside edges of the tent. I was disappointed that directions need to be better. The tent does indeed pack back with all accessories into the carry bag included. I do not camp often, so I wanted a tent that I did not have to waterproof. Because of the claim by Coleman with their Weathertec water proofing, I was more confident in my purchase. I did see the rain bead and run off the tent fabric. But after awhile, the fabric below the rain fly and above the floor tub became saturated and wet to the touch inside. Also, on the very cool nights, this same section became wet to the touch inside the tent. The fabric under the rain fly never was wet and never had condensation in the morning. The ventilation is okay if there is a good breeze, otherwise the sun really heats up the  tent. Since the rain fly is velcroed to tents poles, guywired to ground stakes and attached to tent pole rings with bungee cord, removing the rain fly was not practical. It is really better to be outside the tent to remain cool than to rely on the mesh to keep the tent cool. This was our experience. We had battery operated fans which helped. I returned the tent after one week of use and two rainy nights primarily due to the water issues.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
***Update***
DO NOT BUY THIS OR ANY COLEMAN TENT
We have only used this tent four times, it has been stored properly and never put up wet. The hooks that attach the tent to the poles on one half of the tent have all started ripping off. The other half is completely fine. I contacted Coleman about a possible quality control issue, and they were not only NO help, but also pretty rude about it. I expected to get more than four uses out of a product, and I certainly expected them to at least seem concerned about a possible quality control issue for their products. Shame on them!! I will be researching other brands for my family's camping needs.

We took this tent camping for the first time last Spring, and it worked very well.  It was much larger than what my husband was used to using, but I really liked it.  My son slept in one of the side compartments and we kept our gear in the other one.  In the middle, we had a queen sized air mattress.  We still had plenty of room to move around.  My husband also liked that he could stand up in the middle section of the tent.
It was very simple to set up, two of us set it up in about 20 minutes.  I think one person could set it up on their own, it would just take longer.  We had to break it down in the pouring rain, and even that was pretty easy to do.  Once we got it home and dried it out, everything easily went back into the bag it came in!
I really loved all of the little storage compartments in it.  We took our three year old son, and he loved being able to put his toys in them.
The only issue we had was with a small leak.  The last night we were camping, a really big storm blew through and it was absolutely pouring down rain.  One of the places where the pole attaches to the top of the tent started to drip into the tent.  In a lighter rain, we might not have even noticed it.  And my husband thinks he can weatherproof that spot to prevent us from having the problem in the future. But I should also mention that the bottom of the tent was literally floating on the rainwater in some places, and it did not leak one tiny bit!
All in all, we were very pleased with the tent!  You might just want to waterproof the spots where the tent poles attach to the tent before you take it out--just to be on the safe side.  :0)

title: Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern
description:
Illuminate your outdoor adventures with the Coleman One-Mantle Kerosene Lantern. This durable camping lantern features a one-mantle design that casts up to 784 lumens of high-intensity light. You can light the lantern with a quick match strike and dial in the desired brightness level with the adjustable dimmer knob. The high-temperature globe resists cracking and is protected by built-in spring clips, and the porcelain-coated ventilator helps resist corrosion and rust. A bail handle makes it easy to carry or hang the lantern, and the fuel tank provides a steady base when the lantern is placed on a table or other surface. This outdoor lantern lasts up to 5.75 hours on high with 2 pints of kerosene (sold separately). It comes with a pre-installed #11 mantle and a plastic bottle to help fill the pre-heater. Sometimes, you just can't beat a classic kerosene lantern to light up your campsite. This model is iconic lantern maker Coleman's brightest such device, offering high-intensity light output with the included #11 mantles. It provides up to 5.5 hours of burn time on one two-pint kerosene tank, and -- thanks to an easy-to-operate, single-knob control -- it's as intuitive to use as it is durable. The fuel tank is coated on the interior to hold up to repeated use, and a "porcelain-ized" ventilator resists rust. Includes an automatic tip cleaner. The Coleman Limited Warranty
All Coleman products have a warranty against defects in material and workmanship, and details are included with your purchase. About Coleman Outdoor Products
More than 100 years ago, an entrepreneurial young man named W.C. Coleman had an idea for manufacturing better lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. A century later, Coleman's current catalog features a wide-ranging array of products that make spending time outside a pleasure. The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
reviewText: I love Coleman lanterns. This seems to be built with the quality of old. The mantle did char up after an hour of burning. I am using the high quality clear fuel that Lowes sells for heaters. I bought better quality mantles on eBay. Then I bought a new old stock glass chimney to replace the made in China one with the large made in China lettering. The replacement says made in USA, just like the rest of the lantern. I am now using a bit of Coleman gas mixed in, which some call the Amish mix. The color of the light is a bit yellow when compared to the white color of my Coleman gas lantern.

title: Coleman 8D Square Pack-Away Full Size Lantern
description: Designed for convenience, the Coleman 8D Pack-Away Family Size Lantern features a clever telescoping design. Packed up, the lantern creates a built-in carry case for compact and easy storage and transport. Simply pull up the top to unpack the lantern to its full size (12" tall). The lantern's 15-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube provides an extra-bright, white light, with a reflector that directs light upward and outward for more efficient illumination -- it sheds an impressive 390 lumens of light on High. The light automatically turns off when the lantern is closed, to conserve your batteries. The lantern's bail handle is convenient for comfortable carrying or for hanging. The 8D Pack-Away Lantern runs for up to 24 hours on low or 12 hours on high, with a single set of eight D batteries (sold separately). The bulb and battery are easy to access and replace. Includes a limited 5-year warranty. Lantern measures approximately 12.13" x 6.81" x 6.57" extended, and weighs 2.58 pounds without batteries.
reviewText: This little portable is a neat design and stores away well when traveling.  I didn't give it a higher rating because there are other lights that require 8 batteries that give off more light and cost less.  Of course, they are LED not fluorescent, which is becoming pass.  This is a very good lighting unit but it has been surpassed with more light emitting LED models.
text:
TITLE:
Coleman 8D Square Pack-Away Full Size Lantern

DESCRIPTION:
Designed for convenience, the Coleman 8D Pack-Away Family Size Lantern features a clever telescoping design. Packed up, the lantern creates a built-in carry case for compact and easy storage and transport. Simply pull up the top to unpack the lantern to its full size (12" tall). The lantern's 15-watt fluorescent spiral U-tube provides an extra-bright, white light, with a reflector that directs light upward and outward for more efficient illumination -- it sheds an impressive 390 lumens of light on High. The light automatically turns off when the lantern is closed, to conserve your batteries. The lantern's bail handle is convenient for comfortable carrying or for hanging. The 8D Pack-Away Lantern runs for up to 24 hours on low or 12 hours on high, with a single set of eight D batteries (sold separately). The bulb and battery are easy to access and replace. Includes a limited 5-year warranty. Lantern measures approximately 12.13" x 6.81" x 6.57" extended, and weighs 2.58 pounds without batteries.

REVIEW:
This little portable is a neat design and stores away well when traveling.  I didn't give it a higher rating because there are other lights that require 8 batteries that give off more light and cost less.  Of course, they are LED not fluorescent, which is becoming pass.  This is a very good lighting unit but it has been surpassed with more light emitting LED models.

title: Coleman PowerChill 3000001497 Thermoelectric Cooler with Power Supply (40-Quart), Silver
description:
Perfect for the office, dorm, RV, truck or car, the Coleman PowerChill Thermoelectric Cooler is fully portable, plugging into your vehicle's cigarette lighter. The PowerChill's new motor design provides quiet, long-lasting performance, cooling the contents down to 40-degreeF BELOW the surrounding temperature. The PowerChill is designed with many convenient features, including a patented, adjustable door that can open on the right or left side. The cooler can be used in two positions, either as an upright or a chest cooler. An adjustable shelf divider helps keep things organized inside. The PowerChill Cooler's molded handles make it easy to lift and carry. With a 40-quart interior that holds up to 44 cans of soda or beer in addition to snacks, the Coleman portable PowerChill thermoelectric cooler is ideal for the office, dorm, RV, truck, or car. The cooler is compatible with any vehicle's cigarette lighter. As a result, you can bring the PowerChill pretty much anywhere, from the campsite to the tailgater to your backyard BBQ. The PowerChill is equipped with a redesigned motor that provides quiet, long-lasting performance. The motor cools contents down to 40 degrees F below the surrounding temperature, so you don't have to fret once the ice starts melting in your standard cooler. The PowerChill includes several handy features, including a patented adjustable door that can open on both the right and left side, an adjustable shelf divider to keep things organized, and molded handles that make the cooler easy to lift and carry. The PowerChill even functions in two positions: upright or chest style. The PowerChill measures 15 by 21.75 by 17.125 inches (W x H x D). About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Use it exactly as directions say. I place white tray under fan to keep fan safe n running with plenty of air space. I use it with lid up. I place it in middle seat of SUV n cord plugs into outlet under dash board. Great design n construction. Thrilled. Good price. Buy ac outlet right away by Coleman as well as you need to plan prior to your trip. It needs three hours start up time before you load it n need to use it. It can be used in hotel room as well.

title: Coleman 3-Piece 48 Quart Cooler Combo
description: A variety of coolers for all occasions, the Coleman 48 Quart Cooler Combo is a three piece set that's great for backyard parties, camping, day-trips, and more. A 48-quart cooler, a 5-quart cooler, and 1/3-gallon jug are included in this combo pack, each crafted from solid, lightweight plastic and featuring eco-safe ThermOZONE insulation that retains a cool atmosphere for hours. The coolers are deep enough to keep 2-liter soda bottles store upright; the 48-quart cooler has a capacity for up to 63 cans while the 5-quart cooler can hold six cans. Both coolers are outfitted with a hinged lid. A leak-resistant, rustproof channel makes draining the larger cooler simple. The smaller cooler's lid transforms into a convenient serving tray with four cup holders. The jug features a wide, easy-pour mouth and twist-on cap. Set comes in red or blue (colors subject to availability). You know the name, and you?ll love the company. Coleman is unparalleled in the world of camping and outdoor gear. What started out as an innovation in gas-powered lamps over 100 years ago has grown to an international company that designs and constructs top-quality tents, lanterns, stoves, coolers, sleeping bags, and more. No matter what your outdoor sport: hiking, camping, fishing, hunting, boating, swimming, four-wheeling, relaxing, or tailgating, Coleman has what you need to help get you out there to enjoy it. Includes 1/2-gallon jug, personal cooler, & a 48 qt. cooler. Choose from the available color options. ThermOZONE insulation. Personal cooler reverses to a serving tray. Main cooler can hold up to 68 cans.
reviewText: Purchased this set as a gift to a son who is always borrowing my 30 year old cooler. My old one has experienced being bucked off a horse on a horse back trip in Wyoming and is still working great.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Small. Designed to fit small Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 20.4 x 12 x 4.5 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).
reviewText: This is great for my stove that doesn't have a very good handle and of course there are pockets for tanks on either end.  The stove fits pretty tight.  They could have made the dimensions of the carrying case a little more generous so that you are not stretching the zipper to close the case.

title: Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue
description: Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.

reviewText: I've owned two of these, and both died premature deaths. The first one cracked (okay, let's try and be a little more careful...but it wasn't abused), the second one had a spout that never did thread on right--it was as if it was missing a thread or two. So if you get the right one, I suppose it might suffice for basic, non-rugged duty water carrying, but inspect closely before paying(I guess that means you're out of luck if you buy online).
text:
TITLE:
Coleman Jug With Water Carrier, 5 Gallons, Blue

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman, 5 gallon, blue, poly Lite water carrier, molded in handle & hand grip, fast flow spigot stores on the handle when not in use, non-insulated, polyethylene case resists scratches & dents. Comfortably port water to your campsite, picnic, boat, and beyond with the Coleman 5-gallon water carrier. The carrier features a tough polyethylene construction that's scratch- and impact-resistant, helping it hold up to rough handling. More importantly, the carrier's fast-flow spigot delivers instant water access; no more struggling to tip the carrier each time you fill up your water bottle. The carrier also includes a built-in molded handle and handgrip for comfortable, easy carrying.

REVIEW:
I've owned two of these, and both died premature deaths. The first one cracked (okay, let's try and be a little more careful...but it wasn't abused), the second one had a spout that never did thread on right--it was as if it was missing a thread or two. So if you get the right one, I suppose it might suffice for basic, non-rugged duty water carrying, but inspect closely before paying(I guess that means you're out of luck if you buy online).

title: Coleman Camp Axe
description: Get the right tool for multiple jobs when you're at the campsite with the Coleman Camp Axe. The sharp edge of the durable drop-forged steel head easily splits wood for a campfire or stove, while the flat edge works wonders for driving tent stakes into hard ground. When it's time to pack up, pulling your tent stakes is easy with the notch cut into the head. Durable and well built, the Coleman Camp Axe is perfect for the campsite, wooded terrain, or the backyard. The axe is equipped with a drop-forged carbon-steel axe head that easily splits wood into kindling, along with a forged steel handle and an ergonomic nonslip grip. With the Coleman Camp Axe, you'll be ready for any camping contingency.
reviewText: Blade was dull. Otherwise seems fine for camping

reviewText: Purchased this, thinking I would be able to do some small chopping. I wasn't looking to split logs but essentially cut down firewood into small pieces to start a fire. No way. The blade is worthless. I suppose I could get it sharpened but based on other reviews after I got back from camping I'm just going to return it. In addition to the bad blade, the axe came with a slightly crooked handle which made it awkward to hold. If I was left-handed it may be beneficial because the curve is in that direction however since I'm right handed it's an opposite curve.

reviewText: This little ax is just what I need for backpacking.  It's small, but nice and sturdy.  It works great for driving in the stakes of my tent but when I went to gather firewood I realized the ax had no cutting edge to speak of.  A 3" thick tree would've taken hours to cut down.  If you purchase this ax be sure to buy a sharpening stone as well because aside from driving in tent stakes its pretty much useless out of the box.  Once it's sharpened up it works nicely but be sure to sharpen it before you find yourself in the middle of nowhere with a dull useless ax.

title: Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium
description: Take your Coleman stove wherever outdoor adventures lead you with the Coleman Camp Stove Carry Case, Medium. Designed to fit Coleman 2-burner stoves measuring up to 21.5 x 14 x 4.8 inches, this rugged carry case made from PVC-coated polyester protects your stove during transportation and storage. Strong web handles provide comfort when toting stoves between campsites, while the durable zipper is designed to keep stoves safe and dry. Two propane pouches with drawstrings securely carry extra fuel canisters (sold separately).

reviewText: I saw these last night at a camping goods store and am ordering this one online now.  I wanted to put in a note to tell anyone that is unsure about the sizing that the stove we have recommends this model number for the carry bag (recommended on the box under the flap on one of the sides), but ours is not the grill type one as shown above.  I can see why people are confused.  I suggest you look at the box of the grill/stove you buy and see which bag they recommend you buy to get the right size.  Our stove is #2000007502 Perfectflow Stove.  These look to be very sturdy and will be helpful to transport the stove.  I will update if the one recommended doesn't fit or is not the same as we examined last night.  Update:  It fit perfectly with room to spare and is very well made.  My only problem is that I accidentally bought two of them and only need one.  Guess I will have to send one back.

I saw these last night at a camping goods store and am ordering this one online now.  I wanted to put in a note to tell anyone that is unsure about the sizing that the stove we have recommends this model number for the carry bag (recommended on the box under the flap on one of the sides), but ours is not the grill type one as shown above.  I can see why people are confused.  I suggest you look at the box of the grill/stove you buy and see which bag they recommend you buy to get the right size.  Our stove is #2000007502 Perfectflow Stove.  These look to be very sturdy and will be helpful to transport the stove.  I will update if the one recommended doesn't fit or is not the same as we examined last night.  Update:  It fit perfectly with room to spare and is very well made.  My only problem is that I accidentally bought two of them and only need one.  Guess I will have to send one back.

title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
reviewText:
I like the tent a lot, but there are a few features I wish it had:
1. Clips instead of the sleeves for the poles in the top of the tent (lighter, less likely to get gross over time, easier).
2. Something to hang a light from at the top of the tent.  There is nothing (NOTHING!) anywhere.  Seems an easy addition that wouldn't break Coleman's bank.
3. Some medium between full open windows where any neighbors will see all and the stifling fly completely covering.  There is seriously no in-between, which is silly.
Overall fine for tent camping, but laughable when described as a backpacking tent; waaaay too heavy for that!  Suited my purpose for tent camping, except really do wish I had a bit more options with the fly either being suffocating or exposing my every action to those around me.
In the end, it serves its purpose, but not sure the good outweighs the bad and I probably would look for something else if it had it to do over.

title: Coleman Hooligan Backpacking Tent
description: The Hooligan 2 from Coleman is a 2 person backpacking tent. It features a full coverage coated polyester fly (450mm) that envelops the tent in a cocoon of protection from the harshest weather. A small front vestibule offers some storage space for extra gear and the all mesh body is great for ventilation and stargazing on nights when the weather is clear. The fly seams are sealed at the factory and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against leaks. Set up is with a single pole and thus could not be more simple and quick. Great for beginner backpackers, scouts and those who want to keep it simple.Non Freestanding (1 pole) design is simple to set up and take down.All mesh body for maximum ventilation and comfort and reduces condensation.Zippered back vent in the fly helps to keep the tent comfortable - adjustable through zipper on the back tent wall.Sturdy 11mm (1ea) Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.All mesh body is perfect for stargazing on nice nights.Full coverage fly is perfect for harsh weather.1 Small storage vestibule - 6 sq. ft. - good for boots etc..1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import..
reviewText:
Firstly, for the price this tent is worth it ($70 on amazon prime). I took this tent into a moderately difficult portaging/canoeing trip into the bwca in MN. It was perfect for the trip. Not too heavy and the packed shape isn't too cumbersome given the size and features. I was pleased.  The best feature of this tent is the expansive vestibule of the rainfly. It features a main one near the front and small ones along the other three sides. Helped a ton during some pretty nasty thunderstorms.  Combine it with a heavy duty tarp for the base and you and your gear will be bone dry.

Now for some suggestions:
The main criticism is that the way the tent is pitched has changed a bit and the instructions need to be updated.  The rod for the vestibule is no longer guided through sleeves but through velcro straps.  The same type of straps are used to secure the rest of the rainfly to the main tent rods. Practice before you take this tent out! It will not be intuitively obvious which straps go to which rod.

Second criticism: There is no loop at the top of the tent interior to hang a lantern/light source. This just seems like a missed product oppurtunity but friends have informed me that I could sew a loop on. Not sure if i'm willing to risk the integrity of the tent mesh though.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent for Camping | Red Canyon Car Camping Tent
description: Measuring 17- by 10-feet, Coleman's Red Canyon tent is spacious enough to comfortably sleep eight campers. Ideal for weekend camping trips with family and friends, the Red Canyon features removable privacy walls, allowing you to create up to three separate rooms.
reviewText:
So far, my husband and I, have already managed to go through two of these tents. Though the tent is nice, spacious, and easy to set up it is anything but durable. We have no children and are both into our adult years so the tent sees little, light use and should be expected to hold up more than halfway through a weekend camping.

However, these tents keep breaking in strange ways and strange places despite our gentle treatment. We aren't tough with them by any means, but they just don't hold up. The first one we had the zipper break within the first few hours of set up. Graciously, Amazon sent us a return. Now, a few months after we are out of our return or refund period, we are yet again camping and it broke. This time where the base of the tent connects to the stakes that secure it down.

Unfortunately, I won't be purchasing this tent again, though we did enjoy it while it held together. It's lack of durability even under very gentle, general use is frustrating for the price and make it an unreliable product. I will be trying another brand at this point and hopefully it will hold up better than this particular product did.
