reviewText:
This tent had huge, heavy poles and is not as simple to set up like other tents of similar size and capacity. We had a half day of light to medium rain and water leaked from the bottom seams of both windows. Coleman claims this tent to have "leak-free seams" but that was not the case at all. We had to put our plastic ponchos over both windows (attaching them to the pole clamps) just to make sure water avoids the seams.

Apart from the leak, the tent was sized perfectly for our 4+pet family with room to spare and the porch proved very useful for keeping dirty shoes and wet gear outside and safe. The porch netting also helped prevent most bugs from getting inside the tent area. Best part of the tent was the hinged door. We didn't have to keep zipping up behind us since the hinged door had velcros to close.

title: Coleman Tent for Camping | Elite Montana Tent with Easy Setup
description: Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.
reviewText: Its about as expected.  I fit four teenage boys in this tent and they had enough room.  They really don't want to be anywhere near each other and NEED their space.  This was fine.  But sleep 8?  No.

DESCRIPTION:
Stretch out and relax in style with the Coleman Elite Montana 8-Person Lighted Tent. This cutting-edge camping tent features a cabin-like design with a variety of outdoor technologies to enhance any camping trip. The large outdoor tent can fit three queen size air beds, ensuring your whole family has plenty of room. When the sun goes down, you keep the party going with the six built-in CPX LED lights that provide 100 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by four D batteries (sold separately) or the included CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge and comes with an energy pack that can power virtually anything with a USB slot. During the day, the auto-roll windows neatly roll down when you unzip them and can open at an angle to let in light but not water when it rains. This 8-person tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry. An extended door awning creates a dry space for you to store gear and enter the tent, while the patented hinged door makes it easy to come and go. The heavy-duty tent sets up easily in about 15 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves, a patented pin-and-ring system, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension. Clip on the included rainfly for added wetness protection, or gaze at the stars through the mesh ceiling.

REVIEW:
Its about as expected.  I fit four teenage boys in this tent and they had enough room.  They really don't want to be anywhere near each other and NEED their space.  This was fine.  But sleep 8?  No.

The company crafts coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days, airbeds that are comfortable and won't deflate during the night, a complete line of bright and long-lasting LED lights, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and more.
reviewText: My wife insisted on having a huge tent that would fit a queen sized air bed and a playpen. This fit the bill, so she ordered it. it didn't take long to assemble, but exoframes went out in the 80s for a good reason, I don't know why Coleman wants to bring them back. The rigid door frame was a nice touch and made it easy to get an out of, but I think the screened area is too small to be useful. There are a lot of parts and stakes, but the instructions are fairly clear. Best of all, it fit back in the bag when I was done.

title: Coleman 48 Can Party Stacker Cooler
description: With the Coleman Party Stacker-Inchs unique stackable, color-coded design, you-Inchll want more than one for parties, tailgating, BBQs and more. The Party Stacker features a clever interlocking base and lid, so you can stack multiple coolers with no slipping or sliding -- great for easy transporting and tidy storage. The Party Stackers come in four great colors, so you can separate drinks or dishes by cooler color. The 33-Quart Party Stacker accommodates two cases of twelve-ounce cans or one case of bottles or longnecks plus ice -- and it-Inchs wide enough for a 9-Inchx13-Inch casserole dish. The Party Stacker features tough molded handles for comfortable lifting and carrying, with a sleek, low-profile design for stability. Exterior measures 22-InchL x 13.5-InchW x 11.75-InchH. Made in the USA.
reviewText: The lid isn't great and doesn't seem to stay cool that long.

title: Coleman 24-Can Party Stacker Portable Cooler
description: You'll be the life of the party when you take along the Coleman 24 Can Party Stacker Cooler to your next event. Not limited to canned drinks, the wide base fits 13 x 9 in. serving dishes, so you can easily pack something for everyone. No matter what you bring, the molded handles will make it easy to get it there. To add even more variety, grab a few coolers in different colors and organize your fun. Use one color to chill the adult drinks, another color for the kids, and keep food warm in yet another, so everyone will know where to find their favorite treats. When the fun is done, the specially designed base fits in the lid of other Party Stacker items, making them simple to stack and store until the next glorious get together.

title: Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern
description: Make packing up your lantern as easy as possible with the Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 22 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.
reviewText: I own 3 of these lanterns, and 2 out of 3 of the valves leak slowly when shut off (and did so right out of the box) making it absolutely necessary to remove it from the tank, (or close the tank valve on a 20# tank) when not in use.
text:
TITLE:
Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern

DESCRIPTION:
Make packing up your lantern as easy as possible with the Coleman QuickPack Deluxe Propane Lantern. The 2-mantle design of this lantern creates a steady, bright glow of up to 1000 lumens of light that reaches up to 22 meters away. A quick match strike is all you need to get your lantern started, then simply dial in the perfect brightness with the adjustable control knob. Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.

REVIEW:
I own 3 of these lanterns, and 2 out of 3 of the valves leak slowly when shut off (and did so right out of the box) making it absolutely necessary to remove it from the tank, (or close the tank valve on a 20# tank) when not in use.

Once your lantern is lit, the wide base provides stability when placed on a table, and the bail handle allows for easy hanging. No matter where you go, the PerfectFlow pressure-control technology produces a steady fuel stream and consistent performance in all kinds of weather. When you're ready to head home, the QuickPack storage system helps get you on your way quickly, since the lantern base is also the bottom of your case. Just place the outer cover and you're all packed up until you're ready for your next bright adventure.

title: Coleman Portable Camping Quad Chair with 4-Can Cooler
description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText:
We needed extra chairs for our RV. These were well-rated and inexpensive.
Pros: They come in a carrying case and take up little storage space when folded.  They are more comfortable to sit in than our low-slung beach chairs that these replaced. Morning dew quickly dries off in the sun.
Cons: The drink pocket could be a little deeper and wider to handle a wine glass. The top of the back of the chair offers no support for the neck.
Bottom line: At this price, they're a deal!

title: Coleman Woodsman II Chair
description: The Coleman Woodsman II Chair is a lightweight folding chair perfect for camping, sporting events and more.Unfolded Dimensions: 15.5 in. x 17.5 in. 27.5 in. (39.4 cm x 44.5 cm x 69.9 cm).Folded Dimensions: 22 in. x 17.5 in. x 3 in. (55.9 cm x 44.5 cm x 7.6 cm).
reviewText:
I like it, and I'll use it for short trips, but it's definitely not a backpacking chair. I was willing to lug it on overnight backpacking trips, because I really like having a comfortable place to sit after a long day of hiking. But 3 pounds is more significant that the 1.78 pounds the product description claims when every ounce counts. Even if I was willing to take on the 3 pounds, it is a bit bulky, and would extend wider than my pack if I were to secure it to the outside (I'm a 5'6" female).

Construction seems sturdy. The slight reclining position it puts you in is actually comfortable for relaxing.

title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet
description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter, 13 x 13 Feet, assembles in 3 minutes in 3 easy steps to create a shady area outdoors. This canopy cover is made with UV Guard material that offers protection from the suns harmful rays and features 2-way roof vents to keep the air circulating when the temperature rises. The lightweight preattached telescoping poles can be assembled without having to reach over your head, so anyone can set up the tent easily, while comfort grips lock the shelter into place with pinch-free adjustability. Once the canopy tent is assembled, it offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When its time to pack up, this shelter can be stored and transported in the included easy-pull wheeled carry bag, which fits inside most cars. The Coleman 13-by-13-foot Back Home Instant Shelter provides an easy, convenient shelter in the backyard, at a campsite, sporting event, or wherever you need reliable cover from the sun. The Shelter's sturdy, one-piece steel frame sets up quickly and easily in only three minutes, with Coleman's exclusive push-button release levers so your fingers stay safe during setup and takedown. The heavy-duty shelter canopy gives you an instant hex-shaped shelter with a generous center height of eight feet, four inches and also features SPF 50+ UV Guard protection from the sun's rays. Features:
  • Sets up in less than 3 minutes
  • Heavy-duty 150D canopy with 50+ UV Guard sun protection
  • Sturdy 28mm one-piece steel frame
  • Exclusive push-button release levers take it easy on fingers
  • Hexagon shape for a distinct shelter
  • Wheeled carry bag
  • Footed poles for easy securing and stabilization with steel tent pegs
Specifications:
  • Shaded Area: 13 by 13 feet
  • Footprint: 10 by 10 feet
About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: I bought this back in March, put it up at the beginning of this month (June) and our winds in Vegas have been wicked this year.  First one of the joints gave way so I wired it and then the wind pushed the metal corner arm through the tent fabric.  Just buy a cheap pop up if you are in a windy city.  This was definitely not worth the money for the not even one month it lasted.

title: Coleman Calgary 20 Degree Sleeping Bag
description: The Coleman Calgary Scoop Sleeping Bag is great for cold-weather camping. It measures 33-Inch wide by 78-Inch long, and fits most people up to 5-Inch11-Inch. The unique scoop shape makes a comfortable headrest, keeping your head and pillow off the ground. The Calgary Sleeping Bag is durably constructed of a soft brushed-polyester cover and liner, with 4 pounds of ColeTherm insulation to keep you comfortable down to 40-20Degree Fahrenheit. The Calgary features the QuickCord storage system for easy storage -- no tying required. Coleman-Inchs ComfortSmart technology means your sleeping bag is packed with features to keep you warm and comfortable: ZipPlow plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags; Comfort Cuff surrounds your face with softness; FiberLock prevents insulation from shifting keeping you warm and increasing durability; ThermoLock reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer; Zipper Glide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around corners; and Roll Control locks the bag in place for easier rolling.
reviewText: The fabric is not comfortable and is not what I thought it was.  Like laying in a soft rain jacket.

title: Coleman Converta Cot
description: The Coleman Converta Cot provides comfortable seating at the campsite or in the backyard. Four back position and two foot positions for use as a lounge chair or cot.Unfolded Dimensions: 76 in. x 26 in. x 15.5 in..Folded Dimensions: 27.8 in. x 25 in. x 4.9 in..
reviewText:
I love this chair, not only it is built extremely well, but more so that the front feet area can go downward. I was looking for one of these chaise lounges forever to do this. I need it to be like this because for my small dog to be able to run up a little ramp and climb on the chair with me.

Update: It only lasted 7 months, then the whole complete seat ripped. I do leave it outside all day and reside in Arizona (sunny), but we are into our cooler months now (February). I just a company could make a fabric that can withstand Arizona heat.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: This works awesome. Its easy to tear down and assemble. No tools required. Only drawback is its a little on the heavy side. But if u want the support and durability, I guess its expected. I would definitely recommend to purchase for your camping adventures.

title: Coleman Industrial 30mm PVC Raincoat
description: Stay protected in the elements with the Coleman .30mm PVC Rain Coat. It-Inchs durably constructed of strong PVC-coated polyester to keep you dry. The Rain Coat features a center front zipper with storm flap to keep moisture out. The corduroy collar keeps your neck warm, while adjustable snap cuffs keep your arms dry. Pull the drawstring hood snugly around your face, wear it loose, or remove it altogether -- the choice is yours. There are 2 lower pockets for convenience. The Rain Coat stores compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText:
This raincoat ripped around both arms on the first wear. I duct taped it and it held up for about another 20 wears. That's why I gave it 2 stars. Will not buy again.

Also, I'm 6'1" 190lbs and I got a XL. The raincoat was kinda baggy. I probably could fit into a L, but that would put more stress on the arm joints. Also the jacket went down about 2-3 inches pass my knees.

title: Coleman Men's 35mm PVC/Poly Rain Suit
description: Keep yourself reliably dry and protected from the elements with the Coleman Men-Inchs PVC/Poly Rain Suit. Constructed of durable, waterproof 35mm PVC-coated polyester, the Rain Suit includes a rain jacket and pants. The jacket features a drawstring hood, center front zipper with storm flap to keep out moisture, back and armhole vents for air flow, and two lower pockets. The pants feature a drawstring elasticized waist for a good fit, leg openings with zipper closure & snap adjustment, articulated knees for ease of movement, and 2 convenient slit pockets that give you access to pockets on the pants you wear underneath. The Rain Suit stores compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText: UPDATE... I wore this on a 2 mile walk in a light rain. Though the suit is still new looking and has no rips or tears, I was wet down to my underwear. Not soaked mind you but damp enough that the cell phone in my pocket got a foggy screen and is now sitting in a bucket  of rice. I initially reviewed this on looks alone as I ordered it before the rainy season. I assumed that, because my 20 year old Coleman suit (from when they were still made in New Braunfels TX USA) looked very similar. But my old one is practically shredded and it still offers better protection than this sub standard Chinese suit. It is better than nothing but not by much. save some money and cut a hole in a trash bag. It will work better.

title: Coleman 5-Gallon Beverage Cooler
description: Keep the group cool and hydrated on sweltering days with a Coleman 5 Gallon Beverage Cooler. The side handles make carrying easy, and the screw-top lid stays secure during transport or while lifting this beverage cooler on a table top or bench. Once you're set up at your destination, the entire group can fill their cups from the easy-use faucet and stay refreshed all day long.
reviewText: Like the screw top lid.  Not as thrilled with the flimsy handles though.  Our Scout troop will be using these a lot and have concern the handles won't hold up like the older hard material.

title: Coleman Dunnock Cold Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
description: Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.
reviewText:
Other reviewer said it doesn't roll up. It rolls up fine, it has a built in bag type thing sewn on the bottom. I don't know what he meant because even if you can't roll it tight it doesn't matter, you just get it as tight as you can, rap the flap around and tie the 2 strings. Even has built in nylon handle to carry it. I have not used this in cold weather yet but when I do I will update the review. I did lay in it and it is good size and has one small pocket inside for cellphone or wallet. The zipper zips good. Pretty sure this bag is the replacement to the very popular 'coleman colossal' bag. One difference I noticed is this uses 'coletherm insulation' while the colossal used 'Hollofil-808 insulation' Both carry a 5 YEAR warrenty. The colossal looks a little thicker in the pictures but I never owned  one so I cant be sure. I did own one of the coleman bags with the cotton outside for many years that my grandfather gave me. Back then they seemed like they used a 'tougher' cotton on the outside but maybe its because this bag is still new. I want to add that I bought the very popular Teton Sports Celsius XL bag off amazon and did not care for it. The outside is very slippery like a bubble jacket and prown to ripping. Also if its hot and you try to sleep on top of that bag you will sweat bad. Not with this one.

Update--used it all this winter in my 18wheeler and it worked good. Wouldn't use it in weather below like 45* F, the storage flap has proved to be slightly annoying like the other reviewer said but I haven't cut it off yet. The zipper has snagged only a few times. Changing my score from a 5 to 4 star because the outside cotton is not as tough as the older bag I had, its cheaper material. Amazon has lowered the price to 35$ now. for that price it might be a 5star. Not a big selection of oversized sleeping bags.

title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
reviewText:
Okay, I bought these for a weekend camping trip that was supposed to be 40 degrees fahrenheit at the lowest. During the night there was a sudden unexpected freeze and it dropped down to 25! However, we didn't even notice because we were snug in these sleeping bags. When I woke up, there was a layer of ice on the sleeping bag, but I was still warm!
These are awesome, especially for the price! The color was more of a cranberry/burgundy and they're a little tricky to roll up, but I would definitely recommend this sleeping bag.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: I love this when I'm camping. It is sturdy and moderately easy to store. Wish the stove holder was a solid material instead of mesh.

Ideal for camping at higher altitudes or in cooler months, the Coleman Dunnock Cold-Weather Sleeping Bag helps you sleep comfortably in temperatures as low as 20F. This sleeping bag features Fiberlock construction that keeps the insulation from shifting, reducing cold spots and increasing durability. A cotton cover and ultra-soft cotton flannel liner also keep you warm, as well as a Thermolock draft tube that prevents heat loss through the zipper. The bottom of the bag unzips to provide extra ventilation on warmer nights. The 2-way patented ZipPlow system plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags and frustration. Plus, the Roll Control design and Wrap 'N' Roll integrated packing system make this lightweight backpacking sleeping bag easy to roll and pack. This sleeping bag has a spacious design that can accommodate most people up to 5 feet 11 inches tall and is machine washable for easy cleaning.

REVIEW:
Good size.
But it seemed to be damaged. The fill material did not seem enought. For me not comfortable at all. It semed the bag was not filled properly.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen
description:
Bring a cooking station with full features to your next outdoor gathering with the Coleman Pack-Away Deluxe Portable Kitchen. Equipped with a 39.4 x 19.7-inch food prep area, this portable camp kitchen offers ample space for slicing meats and chopping vegetables. A removable sink easily slots into the prep area for easy cleaning. The side stand accommodates most Coleman stoves and coolers, while hanging hooks, a paper-towel holder, and wire shelves offer additional storage space for plates, utensils, and ingredients. To facilitate late-night cooking, a lantern hook provides a convenient place to hang a light. Setting up this portable kitchen is easy: Simply expand the aluminum frame, snap on the surfaces, and make sure the kitchen's footing is steady. When it's time to pack up, the kitchen folds up and easily fits into a single, self-contained case for easy carrying and storage. With its lightweight aluminum frame and sturdy construction, this folding camp kitchen is built to withstand years of rugged use. Outdoor cooking is a ton of fun, but it's not always easy to prep your meal on coolers, picnic tables, and car tailgates. Enter the Coleman deluxe Camp Kitchen, a complete outdoor food preparation center that fits inside a compact carrying case.


The Camp Kitchen includes a removable dish tub for post-meal cleanup.
The Camp Kitchen is outfitted with a rust-resistant aluminum frame made using heavy-duty channel-extruded aluminum for added strength. More important than the construction, however, is the functionality. The kitchen's main body includes a moisture-resistant MDF tabletop food prep area with plenty of space for chopping onions and shaping hamburger patties. To the side is a wire-grid extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman and other brands of camping stoves at countertop level. No more cooking on the ground or an unstable stump. It's the extras that really make the Camp Kitchen go. The unit offers a handy bottom shelf for storing items like pots, dishware, and bread, along with chrome-plated wire hooks and a spice rack/shelves along the back for mugs, spatulas, and condiments. The Camp Kitchen even includes a removable tub for washing your dishes and a produce bin. And when you're ready to head home, the Camp Kitchen folds up into an easy-to-carry suitcase-style frame that fits easily in most car trunks. Other details include stable leg braces for added support, plastic guards to protect the corners, a lantern hook, a paper-towel holder, and a hand-towel holder. The Camp Kitchen measures 74.5 by 64 by 20 inches (W x H x D) when open and 39.5 by 7 by 20 inches when closed and weighs 24.2 pounds. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Upon arrival I noticed a corner of the counter was chipped. Also, this thing does not fold up very well at all. For the price, I would not buy this again, or recommend it to anyone.

title: Coleman 1-Gallon Jug
description: Keep enough ice-cold water to stay cool and hydrated on sweltering days with a Coleman 1 Gallon Beverage Cooler. The wide mouth top makes for easy filling, including adding ice by the handful. The flip spout makes grabbing a quick drink easy, and it won't snag open to better prevent spills. The bail handle makes carrying easy, and the screw-top lid stays secure, so no matter where you go you'll always have a refreshing drink ready for you.
reviewText: I bought this to use during the day while at work. I do like that it holds a gallon, which is more than enough for most people. I have not had any problems with it leaking, from what I can tell the seals are all still very tight. I have been using this jug for a little over a month now. My one issue with this product is that the lid is not insulated. The inside of the cooler is very  insulated, the walls look to be over an inch thick. The inner insulation, however, is somewhat lost by the lack of insulation on the lid. Because of this, the heat from the day can get into the cooler and warm up the contents rather quickly. The lid also "sweats" because of the lack of insulation, which makes it seem like the whole thing is leaking. Overall, it's a decent product, but nothing special. I would recommend something with an insulated lid if you are concerned with keeping things cold/hot.

My one issue with this product is that the lid is not insulated. The inside of the cooler is very  insulated, the walls look to be over an inch thick. The inner insulation, however, is somewhat lost by the lack of insulation on the lid. Because of this, the heat from the day can get into the cooler and warm up the contents rather quickly. The lid also "sweats" because of the lack of insulation, which makes it seem like the whole thing is leaking. Overall, it's a decent product, but nothing special. I would recommend something with an insulated lid if you are concerned with keeping things cold/hot.

title: Coleman Palmetto Cool Weather Adult Sleeping Bag
description: The Coleman Palmetto Sleeping Bag is a rectangular, cool weather sleeping bag made for camping temperatures between 30 and 50 degrees. Perfect for Family camping!.ThermoLock construction reduces heat loss through the zipper, keeping you warmer.ZipperGlide tailoring allows smooth zipper operation around the corners.QuickCord for easy, no tie closure.ComfortCuff surrounds your face with softness.Commercial Machine washable.
reviewText:
This bag seemed about the right size and weight I wanted when i first unpacked the box.  Immediately the zipper on the outer bag snagged and proceed to break off.  After unrolling it and trying it out, it seems really cheaply made, The zipper on the sleeping bag seems pretty flimsy as well so I expect that to break soon too.  Haven't camped with it yet, but I don't have very high hopes that it will make for a pleasant experience in the 40 degree's it advertises.

I thought coleman was a good name in camping gear?  4 out of the 9 things I just bought for my upcoming trip were Coleman and they were all very junky looking (tent kit, lantern, mess kit, camp pillow).  The Suisse Sport stuff I purchased all seems to be way better made than the coleman stuff.

Edit:

Took it camping once.  The stitching on the bottom zipper ripped so there was a hole by my feet.  By the time I woke up the hole grew to a 10inches long because the stitching just continued to fall out along the zipper.

Coleman has become the worst brand I have had experience with, I tossed this thing in the dumpster, I will never by a Coleman product again.

title: Coleman 8-Person Tent | Instant Family Tent
reviewText:
BUYER BEWARE: We own three Coleman tents and several of their other products generally, their quality is excellent. This particular tent was manufactured incorrectly.

One of the poles was assembled upside down so it won't slide into the groove to either snap into place or collapse. We've used this tent twice and have been able to get it to stand up with the defective pole by using guy lines to compensate. We thought we were doing something wrong but couldn't figure out what.

The real problem comes in taking it down. You can't slide the pole back in. We ended up loosening the screw and hammering it in requiring 4 people to hold it, at 5am, not fun. Instead of being the fastest tent to set up and take down it takes exponentially longer.

You won't find out about this defect until you set it up and try to take it down again. We might be able to take it apart and turn the pole to line up correctly and then reattach it. The problem is that its on the top part of the tent which will require assembling it and standing on two ladders while leaning over the tent. I can't figure out a way to do this safely and frankly, I don't think I should have to.

Coleman and/or Amazon, do you stand by your products?

UPDATE: Coleman is working with us to replace the tent. I'll update here if/when things are resolved.

title: Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot
description: Bring along a perfect relaxation spot on your next camping trip with the Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot. Ideal for sitting, reclining, and sleeping, this versatile camping cot features an extra-wide design that provides ample resting room. It also comes equipped with a convenient removable side table with a cup holder, where you can easily keep drinks, magazines, and other personal items. The steel frame can support up to 300 pounds and is built to withstand season after season of rugged use. Thanks to its folding, portable design and included carry bag, this camping cot fits inside most car trunks and is easy to transport. The Coleman Pack-Away Camping Cot accommodates most people up to 6 feet 6 inches tall.
reviewText: It's very comfortable and makes tent camping great. The reason I didn't score it higher is because folding the legs is not the easiest. The other minor issue is when completely open, the legs tend to collapse relatively easy when no wait is applied.

title: Coleman Adjustable Comfort 30 Degree Sleeping Bag
reviewText: My husband and I had high hopes with this sleeping bag but soon realized that it was not going to work. First of all it was too short for my husband who is 6 feet tall. Secondly, we plan on camping in cold weather and this sleeping bag did not feel warm enough. The sheet was espcially thin. It looked good but was not going to work for us.

title: Coleman Dome Tent with Screen Room | Evanston Camping Tent with Screened-In Porch
description: Enjoy the great outdoors without the bugs inside the Coleman Evanston 8-Person Tent with Screen Room. Great for car camping and extended camping trips, this spacious tent offers enough room for 2 queen-size airbeds and is designed to set up in under 15 minutes thanks to snag-free, continuous pole sleeves and Insta-Clip pole attachments. The tents WeatherTec system features patented welded floors and inverted seams to keep the rain off you and your gear; a rainfly is included for extra weather protection. Extended awnings let you keep the windows open for enhanced air circulation without letting in the rain. The tent also comes with a separate screened-in room for extra ventilation, covered storage for your gear, or an extra sleeping area on warmer, dry nights. Remove the included rainfly to let in more sunlight through the mesh roof on dry days or to gaze up at the stars on dry nights. "
reviewText: On our second use, the fabric where the pole goes through ripped. On our 3rd use, zipper broke and we cant even close the tent anymore. Emailed Coleman multiple times but they won't get back to us. Now the tent is useless and not sure if Coleman will replace this tent even though we still have our one year warranty.

title: Coleman Micro Quad LED Mini Lantern
description: Clever, versatile, and powerful, the Coleman Microburst Mini-Lantern is four lights in one. Give one to each family member at the campsite or nighttime event! The four detachable light "pods" can be used separately -- each has its own On/Off switch, runs for up to 12 continuous hours and sheds 10 lumens of light -- or they can be docked into the base station for a single bright mini-lantern. When docked, the lights are powered by four AA batteries, and run up to 25 continuous hours, shedding 40 lumens of light. Each pod sports a pocket clip, elastic rubber loop, and magnet -- so it can be attached to virtually anything! Four colored lenses (red, amber, blue, and green) store in the base and easily clip onto each pod, so you can assign a color to each person carrying one and easily identify them in the dark.
reviewText: Hardly ever used it and the battery door on the back of one of the lights already broke off and it doesn't stay connected to the unit anymore. It needs a ton of different batteries that have to be replaced at the same time

title: Coleman Cabin Tent with Instant Setup | Cabin Tent for Camping Sets Up in 60 Seconds
description: The Coleman Instant Cabin makes getting camp set up quicker and easier than ever. This instant tent has pre-attached poles that make setting up camp as simple as unfolding, extending, and securing. You can assemble the tent in about a minute and then get started on your adventure. This camping tent is designed with the WeatherTec system that features patented welded floors and inverted seams to help ensure you stay dry if it starts to rain. An integrated vented rainfly offers added protection from the rain and improves airflow without the need for extra assembly. This backpacking tent is made from rugged Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric that stands up to the rigors of the outdoors, so you can use it reliably season after season. This 6-person tent offers enough room for two queen size air beds and comes with integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. And when its time to go home, the camping tent packs away securely in the included expandable carry bag that lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packing.
reviewText: It really does set up in 60 seconds, comes down in about 3 (including rolling and tying) and easily fits back into the bag.  The floor did develop two pinprick holes after setting up on a rocky surface, but they didn't let in water, even after a torrential downpour.  This tent stayed up under extremely windy conditions and in pretty extreme weather. Fantastic for touring when you're camping for only one night and moving on.  I'm really happy with this purchase!

title: Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet
description:
The Coleman Instant Pop-Up Canopy Tent and Sun Shelter with LED Lighting, 10 x 10 Feet, lets you enjoy the outdoors any time of day or night. Great for picnics, barbecues, campouts, and more, this sun shelter is made with UV Guard material that offers UPF 50+ protection from the sun's harmful rays. The lightweight telescoping poles with pinch-free Comfort Grip technology can be extended comfortably without having to reach over your head, allowing you to set up the canopy tent quickly. Once assembled, the canopy offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. When the sun goes down, the fun can keep going thanks to the 6 built-in CPX LED lights that supply 400 lumens of illumination. The lighting system can be powered by 4 D-cell batteries or one CPX 6 rechargeable power cartridge (both sold separately). When its time to pack up, the shelter folds down quickly and fits easily inside most cars. Whether you're camping, dining in the backyard, or hosting a wedding reception, the Coleman Instant Canopy makes an ideal companion. The Instant Canopy is Coleman's most innovative canopy, with a three-step setup that takes three minutes or less to pitch.


The canopy is easy to set up via a 3-step, 3-minute process.
No more fiddling with hard-to-interpret pole assemblies or poorly designed attachment systems. More importantly, the canopy comes with four built-in ultra-bright LED fixtures at the top of the shelter. With light emanating out from the ceiling, you can dine at dusk with full overhead illumination and dance after dark without bringing out multiple lanterns. The light system even comes with a convenient dimmer switch, so you can choose low-light ambience all the way up to bright task lighting. The lighting system operates on four D batteries (sold separately), but is also compatible with Coleman's CPX 6 battery power system. The canopy is sturdily constructed, with a 28mm steel frame and a tough cover that's made using UVGuard fabric, which provides a superior 50+ ultraviolet protection factor. And Coleman added pinch-free Comfort Grips to the frame, so you can protect your fingers during setup and takedown. Just a single squeeze locks or releases the frame for fast setup or teardown. The Instant Canopy offers 10 feet by 10 feet of shade and a center height of 9 feet 4 inches. About Coleman
More than 100 years ago, a young man with an entrepreneurial spirit and a better idea began manufacturing lanterns in Wichita, Kansas. His name was W.C. Coleman, and the company he founded would change life in America. A man plagued with such poor vision he sometimes had to ask classmates to read aloud to him, Coleman saw a brilliant light in 1900 in a drugstore window that stopped him in his tracks. He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come.
reviewText: Bought for a recent group camping trip, and our whole party immediately wondered how we ever camped without it. Don't know how it compares to the newer version (which has different lights I think) but this one is pretty great. One star off for the power pack being kind of cheesy. It's easy for the battery compartment to open a little, which breaks the electrical connection and might make everyone think the batteries are dead. And the stakes are, of course, complete junk. Get some good ones, at least for the four guylines, and you will be much happier.

reviewText: This canopy destroyed our camping trip.  I spent almost $200.00 on this Coleman product and thought it would be capable of blocking rain and being the shelter it claims to be.  Instead, it COLLAPSED after 30 minutes of hard rain and 2 years of use.  We had enjoyed it immensely before the bad weather came.  It works great as a sun blocker!  But this product CAN NOT protect you in a storm.  All of our equipment we were storing under the canopy was soaked and some of it destroyed.  The frame literally snapped in two places!  Our Coleman tent did the same a few years ago while we were asleep in it.  Never buying Coleman products again!  Cheap and unreliable.  Destroyed a $1,000 camping trip and over $400 in equipment (radios, flashlights, solar chargers!!!)  Buyer beware: this canopy is a huge liability.

He inquired about the light inside and discovered he was able to read even the small print on a medicine bottle by the illumination. The lamps had mantles, not wicks, and were fueled by gasoline under pressure instead of coal oil. Soon afterward, Coleman started a lighting service that offered a "no light, no pay" clause--a big step forward for merchants who were burned by inferior products that rarely worked--and drew substantial interest from businesses that wanted to keep their lights on after dark. In the ensuing years, Coleman expanded its product line well beyond lanterns. The company's current catalog is thick with products that make spending time outdoors a pleasure. There are coolers that keep food and drinks cold for days on end, comfortable airbeds that won't deflate during the night, a complete line of LED lights that last for years, powerful portable grills that cook with an authentic open-grill flame, and much, much more. Coleman has truly fashioned much of our outdoor camping experience, and expects to do so for generations to come. REVIEW: This canopy destroyed our camping trip. I spent almost $200.00 on this Coleman product and thought it would be capable of blocking rain and being the shelter it claims to be. Instead, it COLLAPSED after 30 minutes of hard rain and 2 years of use. We had enjoyed it immensely before the bad weather came. It works great as a sun blocker! But this product CAN NOT protect you in a storm. All of our equipment we were storing under the canopy was soaked and some of it destroyed. The frame literally snapped in two places! Our Coleman tent did the same a few years ago while we were asleep in it. Never buying Coleman products again! Cheap and unreliable. Destroyed a $1,000 camping trip and over $400 in equipment (radios, flashlights, solar chargers!!!) Buyer beware: this canopy is a huge liability.

reviewText: Very disappointed in the quality of this tent. The zipper broke almost immediately. I could zip one way, but if I zipped the other way, it came apart. I continued to use it for a while (zipping completely one way), then on my last trip, it broke and would not zip either way. Very embarrassing to get dressed in a tent with the door gaping open! Came home and purchased a different brand. I'm crossing my fingers it will hold up better because I've had it with the Coleman brand. What used to be the quality name in camping has really let me down.

Would only recommend for warmer nighttime temperatures.

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: " The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent is designed for quick and easy setup, so you can spend more time enjoying the outdoors. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you stay dry if the weather turns, while the sturdy frame with Insta-Clip pole attachments stands up to 35+ mph winds. A rainfly with an integrated door awning is included to help keep rain from getting into the tent, and the durable Polyguard 2X double-thick fabric is designed to withstand the elements for reliable use season after season. This dome tent offers enough room for one queen size air mattress and comes with built-in storage pockets to help keep your gear organized. An E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside the tent. The Coleman Sundome 4-Person Tent sets up in only 10 minutes thanks to snag-free continuous pole sleeves and packs away compactly in the included carry bag.
reviewText:
It's a Coleman.  Great entry-level tent that's easy to put up, even for a 10-year old.  Love the bathtub floor (I sure wish tents had that feature when I was a kid, it would have saved me many nights of gear getting soaking wet).  I also love that the pole sleeves are as small as possible and that it relies on pole clips for most of the length of the poles.  That makes getting the poles in and up so much easier than a full-length sleeve.  I wouldn't exactly call them snag-proof but they're a notable improvement over full-length sleeves.

I like the fly on this model, it has one pole that goes front-to-back and does not require much bending.  Similar Coleman models have a smaller fly pole that you have to bend into a U-shape, putting tremendous tension on the fiberglass and subjecting it to splintering.  This model's fly pole is thicker and only has a slight bend so I expect it to last longer.

Biggest complaint is the zipper gets caught in the "zipper cover" all the time and for kids who don't know any better, they just keep pulling until it's completely jammed.  This is not a great design and needs to be revisited.

As with all tents on the market, "4-person" means 4 people sleeping right up on each other with no room for gear.  So this is really a tent for 3 people at most.  We've slept 3 in it comfortably (1 adult/2 kids, 2 adults/1 kid) although we did start to run short on room for gear at some point.

reviewText: This tent was used for a weekend away at a motorcycle rally that my husband went to.  Of course hotel rooms were booked as he decided to go last minute so he got a tent site at a nearby campground.  The tent is compact enough in it's case/carry bag to be able to fit on top of the side bag of his Harley.  It was easily removed from it's carry bag, easy to put up and definitely roomy enough for two.  The bathtub like bottom helped to keep the moisture away from his sleeping bag and other personal items.  With the vents on the tent it was a little chilly (it actually went down to 45 degrees at night in Texas) so this needs to be kept in mind when deciding what you are using it for and where.  It was easy to take down and put it back into it's carry case.  Overall a great tent for the price and good quality!

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
reviewText: Experienced camper. Bought the tent for casual car camping in temperate weather. Set it up last night.  There was some wind.  Nothing epic.  Nothing that isn't within the norm of "peak winds for any given week."  Not part of a storm system.  Within 2 hours one of the rings that anchors the tent floor to the poles and the ground had pulled out of the tent.  Had to spend the night in the back seat of the car.  When I awoke in the morning, all four rings had pulled out.  The pegs (with the rings still in them) were still attached to the ground, but my tent was stuck in a bush 20' away.  This wasn't an old/abused tent.  This was a tent I literally pulled out of the box to set it up.  What a complete waste of money.  The tent was so far away from actually performing that I cannot believe this was just one that slipped through the QA cracks.  This was simply cheap crap.  ...And the last Coleman tent I will ever buy.

TITLE:
Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup

DESCRIPTION:
The Coleman Sundome 6, 10'x10', dome tent is part of the classic Sundome series of tents from Coleman. The Sundome 6 sleeps 6 happy campers and includes a large door for easy entry/exit with a hooded fly to provide ventilation even in rain. A large rear hooded window also provides airflow for cross ventilation. Mesh vents on the roof provide additional ventilation and the rugged 1000D polyethylene floor is a bathtub style (wraps up the sides) for extreme weather protection and has welded seams, even in the corners, to guard against a leaky floor. The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.

REVIEW:
Experienced camper. Bought the tent for casual car camping in temperate weather. Set it up last night.  There was some wind.  Nothing epic. When I awoke in the morning, all four rings had pulled out.  The pegs (with the rings still in them) were still attached to the ground, but my tent was stuck in a bush 20' away.  This wasn't an old/abused tent.  This was a tent I literally pulled out of the box to set it up.  What a complete waste of money.  The tent was so far away from actually performing that I cannot believe this was just one that slipped through the QA cracks.  This was simply cheap crap.  ...And the last Coleman tent I will ever buy.

title: Coleman Sundome Dome Tent
reviewText:
My boyfriend and I took this tent on a practice camping trip before our week long camping trip. It rained pretty hard that night and the tent held up well. Zippers worked, the electrical zipper is a nice addition. However the rain did leak in from that corner of the tent.

We liked that the waterproof tarping on the bottom goes a coupl inches above the bottom of the tent. We had to sleep on some water, and it didn't leak.

I think this is a pretty sturdy tent. Worth the money and I can see us using it for many more camping trips.

The coated polyester fabrics on the fly and walls are rugged and durable. Like all Coleman Sundome tents it is easy to set up or take down and features an Electrical Access Port, a ground vent and an interior mesh storage pocket.Freestanding design is easy to set up and take down.2 windows and roof vents for maximum ventilation and comfort.Mesh roof aids in ventilation and reduces condensation.Shockcorded fiberglass frame for easy set up.Polyester fly is tough and durable and won't stretch when wet.1 zippered Electrical Access Port to run AC power into the tent.1 vented Cool Air Port for increased ventilation and comfort.1 interior storage pocket.Easy convenient clip and continuous pole sleeve construction.Pin and ring pole to body connection is fast and simple.External guy points to keep the tent stable in storms and high winds.Instructions sewn to carry bag so they are easy to find and hard to lose.All carry sacks and stakes included.Includes convenient flashlight loop on the ceiling.Fire retardant.Import.
reviewText:
I do NOT understand all of the positive reviews here. Are we looking at the same tent? How....

Let me start by saying my previous tent of 25 years was a Eureka, and maybe that has spoiled me for all other tents. I bought this thing to replace it when it finally died (word to the wise: if you love your tent, NEVER EVER lend it to other people, I don't care how much you like them. They will kill your tent. This was my fatal mistake, and my tent was put back wet and mold killed it forever. I am DEVASTATED. I LOVED that tent. The places we went!)

I digress. I need a replacement for my beloved Eureka. The reviews on this thing were spectacular, so I bit the bullet and got one from Cabela's (they were on sale for $89 there). NOT WORTH IT!!! IT arrived today, I took it out of the box, put it up, and it's like paper. This tent won't last it's whole year warranty, let alone 25 years like my beloved Eureka did. You want to know how I know? It didn't even last the duration of my putting it up. The fabric on this piece of crap is SO thin that just putting the tent poles through the sheaths on top TORE the fabric. TORE it. I'm not rough n anything - I am very careful and gentle. But my delicate flower like touch RIPPED the sheath through which the poles went, and why? Because they are essentially made of paper.

NO WAY I'd trust this tent in a rainstorm - all the seam sealer in the world wouldn't make this thing sturdy enough to handle any weather. The sun will shred this fabric in a minute.

This is like...this is a disposable tent. I want a tent that will last me, like my beloved Eureka. There is a reason they have lifetime warranties. Lesson learned, I won't waste my money on anything else again. I'm willing to spend three times as much on a tent that will last a million times longer.

What a piece of junk. If you just want a crap tent that you can take camping once, by all means throw away your hard-earned money on this embarrassment to tents. If you want something that will last you and protect you and give you thousands of happy memories over the next couple of decades, get a Eureka.

title: Coleman Dome Tent for Camping | Sundome Tent with Easy Setup
description: Sundome 2P Dome TentFeatures:Up to 33% more water resistant verses a comparable standard Coleman tent Weathertec System patented welded floors and inverted seams help keep water outSnag-Free continuous pole sleeves for easy setup Ground Vent for increased ventilationTwo windows for great ventilation and viewsDoor awning helps keep the rain out E-Port makes it easy to bring electrical power inside your tent Storage pocket to keep your gear organized Expandable carry bag lets you tear away the rip strip for easy packingSpecifications:Doors:1 D-DoorRainfly:PolyguardTent:Polyguard,Polyester Mesh 68DPoles:FiberglassWeight: 7.2 (3.3 KG)<br/
reviewText: I used this tent last year for a hiking trip. It's not the lightest weight tent I've seen out there, but for a budget minded person, it works well.  The tent isn't the best quality, but it works well in fair weather conditions.  I was the only person in my tent, and with my gear, it was about all you'd want to fit.  If you have two people wanting to fit in this tent, you may need to keep most of you gear outside.  The tent is very easy to setup and take down.  It packs away into a small space, and has a nice "floor mat" that you can use when you go in or out to put your shoes on.  In colder weather, this tent isn't much more than a wind shield, and even there, with the vent on the back side that you cannot close, its really hard to keep warm. Otherwise, it's a good starter tent for camping/hiking.

title: Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory
description: The Coleman 4-Person Instant Tent Rainfly Accessory lets you enjoy added protection from bad weather while increasing airflow inside your tent. Coleman tents are sealed strongly to prevent water from entering, which means campers can sometimes lose airflow when it starts to rain and they zip up the windows. Thats why this rainfly accessory is designed to fit over the top of 8- by 7-foot Coleman 4-Person Instant Tents (sold separately), providing added rain protection while giving you the ability to open windows without having to worry about rainwater getting inside.
reviewText: Perfect! once I ordered the right size!

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: Good lightweight bag.  Comfortable shoulder strap.  Almost perfect except netting on each side is not large enough to hold a 16 oz water bottle.

title: Coleman 9-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: Keep food and drinks cold with the Coleman Soft Cooler. This compact, portable cooler bag holds up to nine cans and offers plenty of space for a family picnic or game-day snacks. Heat-welded seams in the zippered main compartment prevent leaks and maintain a cold environment, while the removable hard plastic liner fits snugly inside the soft cooler and allows you to easily dump out any ice melt. When not using the hard plastic liner, you can use ice substitutes to keep contents cold and dry. The cooler bag's easy-clean interior has an antimicrobial lining to resist formation of mold, mildew, fungus, and odor. A zippered front pocket, pocket under the lid, and two side mesh pockets make it easy to carry water bottles, snacks, silverware, and other accessories. You can also secure items to the top with the convenient bungee cords built into the lid. An adjustable shoulder strap lets you comfortably carry the cooler anywhere you go.
reviewText: we ordered both green and blue.  It is as described.  We use it daily.  Great so far.

title: Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels

It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: Needed a wheeled portable cooler for our trip to the NJ shore and this one fit the bill. I love the removable liner since I can move things in and out of the house without sand everywhere.  It rolls well the handle is sturdy and it keeps items pretty cool considering ambient air temps were in the low 90s

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It holds 16 cans inside, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: For the money, this thing is quality!!  I put a cooler shock dry shell pack with a towel (because it got too cold!) and my granddaughter's Calciworms in it to keep them at 50-60 degrees.  (they're for her bearded dragon)  It's a neat, inexpensive way to keep them for a month or more and saves me money. I'm really pleased...The worm company recommened a wine cooler!!! I think those are way more expensive!!!

reviewText:
Not your Dad's cooler, that's for sure.

Needed something for the gym that would keep my post-workout meal cool here in Vegas. This cooler is garbage and it cheaply made. The inside hard plastic liner is PAPER THIN - not THICK like the old school ones used to be. The material on the outside doesn't insulate well enough to keep anything cool on the inside. The zipper on top to seal the whole thing, after just a few weeks of use, is already getting stuck when trying to close. The strap (which I'm about to just cut off with scissors is annoying) is actually stitched on - you can't remove it! What the hell?

What's happened to Coleman over the years? They used to produce quality stuff. Too much outsourcing to China apparently to bring the cost down.

Anyways, don't waste your money or time. I'm the hunt for a new one now.

title: Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner
description: The Coleman 16-Can Soft Cooler goes with you to picnics, BBQs, camping, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. Sporting an adjustable shoulder strap, the cooler is soft on the outside for comfortable carrying, and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect your food from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides antimicrobial protection, to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText: First one I bought I struggled to unzip it the first time and once I did was greeted with a hole in the liner right above the zipper track.  Grrr.  I immediately returned and asked for a replacement.  Second one arrived (two days later, amazon is great), and I carefully as slow as I could trying my best to navigate around the corner of the top unzipped; snag!  Another hole :(. I took the hard white container out to see if the bag maybe would open and close easier, a little bit easier but definitely could forsee the zipper snagging and ripping the lining even more.  Feeling around the upper lining just above the zipper track (on the inside) I could feel a hard wire pushing forward.  Why??  This is just sooo poorly designed!  Why is this product getting such good reviews??  So I will be returning this one too and continuing my search for a cooler.  If you don't mind your bag getting holes in the liner than it seems fine otherwise.  I did not test to see how long it kept things cool but what do you expect from something small with a zipper?  If anyone knows a good cooler bag let me know ... I am looking for a zipper cooler with side pockets.  Easy sounding but hard to find thus far.
