title: Coleman Quickbed
description: Get a restful nights sleep in your home or tent with a Coleman QuickBed. Relax on 32 comfort coils that provide superior support while the soft top offers luxurious comfort for sleepers on the go. With durable PVC construction and the Airtight System, your airbed is guaranteed to keep its customized firmness all night long. The integrated Wrap 'N' Roll Storage System allows you to fold, roll and store the airbed quicklymaking cleanup easy. W.C. Coleman was a young salesman taking a stroll after a hard days work selling typewriters when he spotted a new type of lamplight in a drugstore window in Brockton, Alabama. This new light burned with a strong, steady white flame and was fueled by gasoline. The standard lamp of the era burned kerosene and produced a smoky, flickering, yellowish light. W.C. was stricken with very poor eyesight, and was very interested in this new, steady white light that enabled him to read even the smallest print in books and on medicine bottles. Coleman saw potential in the new light, and through his vision a new company was born that would put Americas farms and ranches in a new light, and would eventually make his name synonymous with outdoor fun.
reviewText:
I took this bed on my last camping trip though I didn't buy this on Amazon.

I wish I had really checked it out (out of the box) before taking it up the mountains.  I bought a tent (please see my review) here on Amazon that stated that it would fit a Queen size air mattress, which is what the box stated it was.

Once the tent and mattress were set up I noticed that there was still half the floor left, not believing my camping partner that it was a full size matress I showed him the box that stated that it was a queen, later agreeing with him that it was indeed a full size put in the wrong box.

The matress itself (states in the instructions) loses air due to the fabric stretching, we had to refill (top it off) each night (slightly irritating).  The feel of the mattress itself reminds you of a suade type of material.  The "wells" (for lack of a better word) in the top of it are a bit uncomfortable (I would recommend sleeping on something else on top of the matress (we used sheets).  After getting home we used a vacuumn to clean of any debris left behind, we also tried to clean off marks with dawn and wet rag, sorry to say it didn't clean up well.  Once dried thoroughly we tried to roll it up into it's "roll up material" , it didn't center well to roll it per the store away instructions nor did the ties match up well for perfect tieing.  We ended up doing the best we could but also storing it in a plastic container.

All in all, it was better than sleeping on the ground, but not really big enough for two adults comfortably.

title: Coleman Soft Plush Top Inflated Quickbed
description: Take the comfort of an indoor bed with you when you sleep outside on the Coleman Queen Airbed Folding Cot with Side Tables and 4D Battery Pump. Connect the included 4D pump to the Double Lock valve for easy, leak-free inflation. Once you find the perfect firmness, the AirTight system will help keep it that way through the night, since it's factory-tested to be leak-free. The ComfortStrong coil system provides all-night support. It is more than a place to sleep with 2 side tables with cup holders to easily place your drink, a snack, a magazine, or personal items on both sides of the bed. A sewn-in cover keeps the airbed in place on the strong steel frame. When youre ready to pack up, the Double Lock valve lets you deflate the airbed just as easily, and it folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText: I love coleman stuff.  But my first one came and had a leak somewhere.  I had to get a replacement.  Luckily Amazon is amazing and helped me replace it for free.  The new one is still in the box.  I'm hoping it doesn't leak also.  Otherwise, it's a GREAT air mattress.  Very comfortable and the new valve style is really cool!  You don't have to rush to plug it back up.  It is more of a check valve so when nothing is inserted it plugs itself.

title: Coleman Packable SUV Quickbed
description: The Coleman SUV Quickbed is sized just right to fit in the back of most SUVs (including Tahoe, Suburban, Xterra, Xplorer, Trailblazer and more) -- and it's equally convenient in your tent or guest room! The SUV Quickbed measures 70" long by 41" wide when fully inflated -- or adjust it down to 63" long to fit your SUV. Coleman's ComfortSmart coil system features 45 coils for a firm, reliable sleep; the Quickbed is topped with soft suede for luxurious comfort. It's durably constructed of heavy-duty, puncture-resistant PVC. The Quickbed is easy to inflate and deflate, thanks to Coleman's extra-wide Advanced Double Lock Valve. Like all Coleman inflatable products, this Quickbed features Coleman's Airtight System, guaranteed not to leak. And the built-in Wrap 'N' Roll storage system makes packing and storing quick and easy.
reviewText: When this item was smaller than I expected, Coleman declined to let me return it. I guess it was what I deserved by going by the size of our SUV instead of reading the specs, but in this world of mail order, easy returns are important if the whole system is going to work. This was the second coleman product I have had a problem with, and I wonder if their quality is falling off.

title: Coleman ComfortSmart Cot
reviewText: Quite comfortable and wide using the foam mat. Quality is good too. I like a firm bed mattress so sleeping on this Coleman cot didn't phase me in the slightest. My only complaint if that's what you could call it is that the surface material of the cot - something nylon like - is a real dog hair magnet. If you have a pet and don't want to see dog hair just keep a handy vacuum near by.  On the other hand, I live in an state that has extremely dry air, so if you're not someone who does, then you may not have this annoying issue. Otherwise, I'd buy it again if I had a use for another one.

title: Coleman RoadTrip LXX Portable Propane Grill, Black
description: Show off your grilling skills on camping trips or at tailgate parties with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Portable Propane Grill. This outdoor grill combines a collapsible, portable design with convenient features that facilitate grilling in tough conditions. The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.
reviewText: Uneven heat and handle is too short.  Wish I had gotten something different.  I was really excited, as I had read so many positive reviews, and videos on how to convert this to LP from your already regulated rig line.  Sadly, after using this on multiple trips, I wish I had gotten something else.  It cooks very unevenly, and every-time I try to use the wheels, the lower table top pops out and drags/scrapes on the ground.  Just overall poorly designed and executed.  I've bought and used different swap tops for it, and it doesn't help.  All I've come up with is leaving one side on high to seer meats, and then placing them on the other side that is off (as low is still too hot) to slow cook.  Not what I wanted or was expecting.

reviewText: I like the grill for portability and features, but it has some major flaws. For one, if you are thinking about hooking this to a camper quick connect supply or a hose adapter on a 20# propane tank forget about it. It will work, just know you will need about $50 worth of adapters and may need to cut and splice propane hoses which most folks have no business doing for safety reasons. Secondly, just know this grill has a very low profile lid which makes it hard to grill in foil pans and impossible to warm up anything in a saucepan without having the lid wide open.

The grill's individually adjustable dual burners each blast out 10,000 BTUs of heat, giving you enough cooking power to prepare a wide range of foods, from grilled veggies to thick slabs of steak. The Swaptop interchangeable design allows you to switch out the grill grates for a griddle top or stove grate (both sold separately), while the Instastart ignition lets you light the burners by simply pushing a button. Ideal for summer cookouts, this portable gas grill also excels in chilly conditions thanks to PerfectFlow technology, which helps ensure steady heat output. Sliding tables on both sides of the grill provide easy access to ingredients and utensils and can be tucked away for easy transport and storage. With its 285-square-inch cooktop, this grill offers a large cooking surface area without sacrificing portability. Its collapsible stand sets up in seconds and folds up for compact storage, while its large handle and wheels make the grill easy to transport. And thanks to its porcelain-coated cast iron grates and removable grease tray, this camp grill is easy to clean.

REVIEW:
We ordered the first one, it had a broked handle right out of the box, sent it back for an exchange. The new one came quickly, it also had a broken part. The ventury tube was broken off. Sent that one back for refund. Bought its little brother the LXE, flawless. I think the broken handle was a shipping problem but the broken burner tube was a manufacturing fault

title: Coleman Cooler Stainless Steel Hinges and Screws
description: For replacement on most Coleman Coolers with plastic hinges as well as Coleman steel Coolers made after 2000.
reviewText: The hinges on my Coleman cooler did not fail at the flex point, but the screws pulled out of the plastic. I could have reused the hinges and simply moved them over an inch or two, but I decided to replace with the stainless steel ones instead. The problem that I see is that the screws are simply screwing into very thin plastic. It is extraordinarily easy to over tighten the screw and simply strip  it out of the plastic. The screws that pass through the body of the cooler could easily be replaced with through bolts and nuts, however you cannot do this for the ones that screw into the lid so just be very careful when screwing in that you do not over tighten and strip out the plastic. You do not need to drill holes, you can simply just press firmly with your screwdriver and the screws will bite right into the plastic. Once you get them started this way, then be very careful to apply only minimal torque and do not over tighten.

title: Coleman Compression Sack
description: Whether you're packing or storing your gear, reduce your bulk with a Coleman Compression Sack. Its unique design compresses large sleeping bags or other soft items with three straps that tighten around the outside. When you're ready to go, the sturdy web handle on the bottom makes one-handed carrying simple.
reviewText: When i went to put my bag in the sack the middle strap apparently was either defective or of terrible quality that the yellow strap itself tore at the buckle. I am very disappointed in this product, Coleman is usually very good. I do not recommend this sack to anyone and will not buy another.

title: Coleman Compression Sack
description: Whether you're packing or storing your gear, reduce your bulk with a Coleman Compression Sack. Its unique design compresses large sleeping bags or other soft items with three straps that tighten around the outside. When you're ready to go, the sturdy web handle on the bottom makes one-handed carrying simple.
reviewText:
Would give 1 star if it wasn't so cheap. Fell apart with minimal use. Seams especially near the straps gave way. The straps themselves are double stitched but could use reinforcement on the excess bit. The stuff sack material itself is very thin which inherently makes it very light and perfect for backpacking. Maybe one or two trips and this will fall apart. Getting all the seams double stitched with thicker thread.

Will steer clear of this. Already ordered one from REI which is about the same in cost but hopefully better in quality.

title: Coleman 3000002009 28Qt Xtreme
description: Take the new, stylish performance of a Coleman 28 Quart extreme 3 cooler with you to the party-at the tailgate, BBQ or campsite. While your friends revel in the sleek new look, you'll enjoy the added improvements that better fit your lifestyle. The redesigned 28-quart cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright and large enough to hold up to 36 cans. Two molded cup holders help prevent spills, and the Have-A-Seat lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) so you'll always have a place to sit and rest. Inside the cooler's walls, extra Thermo zone insulation and extreme technology holds the cold for three days in temperatures up to 90 F (32.22 degree C), and still performs amazingly on hotter days. The foam is also free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone. When the fun is done, the textured EZ-Clean top makes washing your cooler simple. title: Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove
description: Cook breakfast, lunch and dinner on the legendary durability of a Coleman Triton Series 2-Burner Stove. Once this propane camping stove starts to heat up, PerfectHeat Technology gets the temperatures just right, WindBlock Shields keep the wind from blowing the flames around and the PerfectFlow Pressure Control System keeps the heat steady on each of the high-performance 11,000-BTU burners. The spacious cooking surface is large enough to fit a 10-in. (25.4 cm) pan and 12-in. (30.48 cm) pan at the same time, and independently-adjustable controls allow you to set your heat anywhere from simmer to boil. The two burner camp stove is also efficient, with a 1-hr. runtime on high using a single 16.4-oz. propane cylinder. When the fun is done, removable heavy-duty, nickel-chrome grates make cleaning easy, and the built-in handle will have you carrying this lightweight stove effortlessly.
reviewText: Works great and the burner control works great but the external pipe seems to be a little flimsy. Will have to see how long it lasts.

title: Coleman Xtreme Cooler, 28-Quart, Dark Gray/Orange/Light Gray Overmold
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time, and make the fun last longer, when you bring a Coleman 28 Quart Xtreme Cooler. Xtreme technology uses an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls to keep your items cold for up to 3 days. Large enough to hold 36 cans, you'll have plenty of space for refreshments for the entire party. Whether you're headed on a long weekend camping trip, a roadtrip, or out to a BBQ, the comfort-grip no-crush handles make getting to your destination easy and comfortable. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid will ensure you always have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid will keep your cold drink close while you enjoy the day.
reviewText:
I have mixed feelings about this cooler.  I have a big one and love it, but wanted a smaller one for weekend campouts so i was excited about a 28 Quart.  First the good,  I LOVE the size, and holding a 2 litter great.  I freeze 3 or 4 full of water and it keeps everything cool without getting wet all weekend. 3 days plus and I still have ice that way, and you can drink the cool water if need be. My pic is on day 3.

The bad.  The handle is CRAP. Complete crap! Someone thought a single handle with a soft grip would be nice.  To get the grip, they carve out over HALF of the plastic and destroy the structural integrity of the handle.  It flexes in the middle under weight and i'm sure it will break eventually.  This cooler deserves two side handles like the other Xtremes! Coleman fix it!!!  In the same size Marine cooler they left the handle solid and its much stronger.

reviewText: I hate writing negative reviews, I really do, but we are sorely disappointed in this cooler.  We have used it for a week straight and the temps have only gotten over 90 one time and we kept it in the shade all the time. Not once has the ice lasted until the next day but I will say the water stayed cold for 2 days at a time.  The first 2 days we gave it a leeway thinking that once the products were cold inside it would last longer but no, the ice melted in a few hours and left a pile of water.  We have an old junky cheap cooler that works better than this (but it's broken). Maybe we just got a bad one I don't know but I want people to be warned, if you get this make sure you're not depending on it for 5 months straight and then are disappointed once on your trip.  We travel constantly for our business and were hoping this was as good as people said but we were extremely disappointed.

reviewText:
Does keep cool well over time but I have a big issue spending this much on a cooler and then discovering that rainwater comes in through the overhang on the lid, the seemingly tight seal, and into the cooler in copious amounts.  I gathered about 4 inches in one night of rain.
It looks impossible for water to do this from the appearance, but, there it was the next morning- a several inch pool inside.
Couldn't a good company with a long history of making these things learn to prevent this?

Don't trust it.  Expect it to fill with water.

title: Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow
description: Enjoy additional support and cushioning with the Coleman Self-Inflating Camping Pad with Pillow. This camping sleeping pad features a comfy tufted design that adds an extra layer of padding between your sleeping bag and the ground. Equipped with a free-flow valve, the sleeping pad self-inflates with no air pump needed. Simply roll it out and open the valve, and the pad will begin inflating. When it's time to pack up, reopen the valve and push the air out using the compression straps. Thanks to its durable, weather-resistant polyester shell, this camping sleeping pad can endure night after night of use.
reviewText: Heavy and very plastic-y feeling and smelling.  After airing it out and leaving it inflated for a week or so, it finally smoothed out and the small faded some.  To call is "self-inflating" is true only if you want it to self inflate about half-way, and then you finish it off by blowing it up, like you have to blow up the so-so pillow.  I like Coleman stuff, but this one is pretty so-so.  In fact, that sums up this pad in my book.  Just so-so.

title: Coleman Sportsman Waterproof First Aid Kit, 100-Piece for boating
description: Campers, hikers, hunters, bikers, boaters, and other outdoor enthusiasts can rest easier with six first aid kit options available under the Coleman brand - the most trusted name in outdoor equipment. Each kit contains components that are most used and useful when you or your family needs medical attention away from home. Colemans Sportmans Waterproof First Aid Kit contains: 20 - Bandages 1" x 3", 20 - Small Bandages, 8 - Butterfly Bandages, 16 - Spot Bandages, 5 - Knuckle Bandages, 8 - Antiseptic Wipes, 4 - Sting relief Wipes, 2 - Large Safety Pins, 2 - Small Safety Pins, 4 - Antibiotic Ointments, 1 - Razor Blade, 2 - Rubber Gloves (1 pair), 6 - Gauze Pads 2" x 2", 1 - 1/2 x 2 1/2 Yards Adhesive Tape, 1 - Waterproof Case.
reviewText: The kit has plenty of essentials but the waterproof box itself is not too sturdy.  The first time I opened it, the metal clamp on one of the sides fell off.  When I inspected the item, it turned out that the plastic that holds it in place broke.  I would have expected camping gear announced for outdoor uses and waterproof, would be sturdier than that...

title: Coleman 75-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels
description: Easily transport cold food and beverages with the Coleman 75-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler with Wheels. Perfect for sporting events and outdoor activities, this wheeled cooler comes equipped with 2 recessed, heavy-duty wheels that make transport easyeven when the cooler is filled to the brim. Simply extend and lock the telescoping handle, close the hinged lid, and wheel the cooler wherever you need to go. Premium insulation along the lid and walls helps ensure your items are kept cool, delivering ice retention of up to 5 days in temperatures as high as 90F. With enough capacity for up to 118 cans, the cooler provides ample space for barbecue ingredients, perishable food, drinks, and more. When it is time to go, the leak-resistant drain allows you to easily empty excess water without tilting the cooler. And thanks to its integrated cup holders, this wheeled cooler helps you keep a beverage always close at hand.
reviewText:
Love the size, the extendable pull handle is sturdy. The side carry handles (and front) look nothing like this one in the image on Amazon - the one I got has flimsy white slide-type handles that look and feel like they could snap at any time. See image posted.

Unfortunately, it doesn't do the "keep it cool" job nearly as well as our 15yr old Rubbermaid with a split-top lid and the same 4 cup holders (and way better, much sturdier side handles)... The lid on this Coleman feels thin (although it is sturdy enough for an adult to sit on top) and the whole lid has to open to retrieve something, it just wasn't keeping ice from melting as fast as our older cooler. We brought both to Lake Havasu recently, to compare them, with avg temps about 90-95*.

Just don't think this one will do the job or last nearly as long as the Rubbermaid - but it was worth a shot.

The tent can accommodate two queen sized beds, and with a center height of 6 feet, there's plenty of room to move around and get situated. Great for a weekend trip with the whole family.

title: Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair
description: Bring a comfortable seat with you to the campsite with the Coleman Broadband Mesh Quad Camping Chair. Ideal for camping, picnics, or backyard barbeques, this versatile folding chair offers premium comfort in a roomy yet portable design. It features a breathable mesh back that allows the breeze to cool your back on hot days, plus an integrated mesh cup holder that keeps a refreshing beverage within your reach. Designed to ensure your comfort, the chair features an extra-wide 21.3-inch seat that offers plenty of room to lounge and adjustable arms that let you customize the chair to suit your preferences. The strong steel frame can support up to 250 pounds and withstands seasons of heavy use. And thanks to its collapsible design and convenient carrying bag, this folding chair is easy to store and transport.
reviewText:
Nice solid chairs, pack relatively small, relatively light.
No thrills, but also no problems.
They do what they are supposed to do, and do it well.
And cheap.
I'm happy with them - warmly recommended.

title: Coleman Weathermaster II Screened 10 Person 16 X 10 Tent with Hinged Door and Autoroll Windows, 7 Feet Tall and Fits 4 Queen Air Beds
reviewText: This is a HEAVY  tent to move around and really ANNOYING to setup. I'm going to return it tomorrow.  I just purchased this tent on sale with high hopes. I need a tent that's large enough for my husband, myself two boys and a dog. Thank god I did test run setup in the back yard before our camping trip. The directions are clear and easy to follow, however you really need 2 people to assemble the tent. I didn't like the fact that the tent ceiling setup is separate from the walls. I much prefer a dome style tent that can be put up quickly and taken down easily. I am also not pleased with the quality. The bag that it comes had a zipper that kept jamming and a seam that was splitting. Not a good sign for the tent itself. And a total pain since you would rely on that outer bag to store & move the tent around. The REI Base Camp 6 by comparison was a cakewalk to set up and really good quality but is not quite large enough. I ordered the Kelty Discovery 8 man tent and hope that one works.

title: Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge
description: Power all your Coleman CPX 6 gear with the Coleman CPX 6 Rechargeable Power Cartridge. This rechargeable battery kit allows you to make any Coleman CPX 6 product rechargeable, helping you save money on disposable batteries and minimize waste in landfills. Each kit includes a rechargeable battery, a 12-volt adapter for convenient vehicle recharging, and a 120-volt adapter for use with conventional AC outlets. Built to resist impacts and water, the battery features a rugged design and comes fitted with a rubber plug to protect against dirt and debris. It recharges quickly via either a vehicle or home outlet. And thanks to the power cartridge's charge indicator, you'll know exactly when the battery is fully charged.
reviewText: The charge does not last as long as I would like.  It is great for a weekend or over-night trip.  I need a device for a week in the Rockies, so I don't think this will suffice.  I found a solar device that may do the trick.  I'll let you know.

With a tough 200D nylon shell and Cross Tech Flotation Foam, you're sure to be wearing it when you paddle season after season.

REVIEW:
Very nice and fits well

title: Coleman Propane Grill | RoadTrip LXE Portable Gas Grill
description: Stop manhandling those heavy, bulky backyard beasts and show off as the grill master with the Coleman RoadTrip LXE Propane Grill. It's easy to tote around and set up. Just fold it up, roll it with one hand to your favorite cooking spot and set it up in a matter of seconds with the lift and lock system. The grill comes equipped with durable cast-iron grilling surfaces. (Coleman RoadTrip pot support and griddles are available separately.) When you're ready to grill, push a button to ignite the InstaStart matchless lighting system. Cook for more than an hour on one propane cylinder with 20,000 BTUs of power on 285 sq. in. When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.

reviewText:
A very handy grill.
We use it every trip in our motor home.
Seems to get dirtier than most other grills but it's not hard to clean.
Knocked off 1 star because the propane bottles do not line up correctly with the bracket that holds them.
I'm not going to use the expensive small bottles after I run out so it won't matter.
I bought a hose so I can use a regular propane tank.

reviewText: The left burner does not ignite via the igniter. I'm having to use a manual lighter for that. I can't find anything in the manual as to how to fix this issue. Also, very difficult to clean after cooking food that has grease, such as hamburgers. The grease gets all over in the bottom and there's no way to take out the bottom tray and clean it.

When you're ready to leave, it folds down small enough to fit in the back of most car trunks.
reviewText:
It's a decent enough grill.  Really lacks any control over flame.  High might as well be "Volcano" and low might as well be "Afterburner"

That's fine.. my first cook on it went ok, once I got a feel for it, the second was better.

What kills me is the migraine inducing rattle when the thing is in a car being moved.  The grill plates rattle, the wheels rattle, the lid rattles..Fine..  I remove the plates, bungee the lid down, and tape the wheels in place.

Then the hinge hardware rattles too.  And it's all tightened down.  Sigh.  Call Coleman, it's normal for it to do that.

So, it's in the box.. Waiting for UPS to pick it up.

reviewText: Seems nice BUT there is a warranty IF you register your product on line - GOOD LUCK with that.  After about 20 minutes on hold I got a person who said she would send me an email - they want pictures of the grill and a copy of the receipt.  I'm sure the computer wizards could do this but being 76, I don't have a clue. So when it fails and or needs parts I guess I'll just throw it away and buy a new one.  So much for CUSTOMER SERVICE.

title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
reviewText:
We bought ours at Big 5 - it was yellow. This appears to be the same with instant set-up and 6-person capacity.

Our first mistake was not trying to assemble it at home before taking it into the wilderness. We bought it for the instant setup, as my husband was doing this solo while I arrived on a later boat.

One minute turned into THREE HOURS of setup due to poor Quality Control at the factory. Some of the pieces that were supposed to be together weren't, so he couldn't set it up in a minute or two as promised. He's an engineer, so he figured out the problem fairly quickly but not the solution. Two poles were not joined where they should have been. And when he did attempt to join them, he didn't do it right at first. (This is not a simple old-school join poles together problem.) He finally figured it out and raised the tent, but a screw did pop out and he couldn't put it back in where it came from, because it was broken and wouldn't stay.

Then, a mild gust brought the entire tent down. This is a design flaw, part of the joints that make the tent an instant-set tent. If it blows slightly the wrong way, the whole thing collapses. This happened to us twice. Our only saving grace was that the mild gusts happened in the afternoon and not late at night.

One other mentioned that the tent is hard to zip around the corners. I agree. Pretty flimsy.

I am extremely disappointed. Coleman used to make good tents.

title: Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent
description: Great for festivals and car camping, the Coleman 2-Person Pop-Up Tent sets up in as little as 10 seconds, letting you get back to the fun. This camping tent is uniquely designed with preassembled poles, allowing the tent to pop into place in a snap. The taped floor seams keep you dry even when the ground is soaking wet, while the multi-position rainfly allows you to find the perfect balance of protection and ventilation. This instant tent offers room for two camp pads and comes with two integrated storage pockets to help you keep small items organized. This 2-person tent folds flat for simple storage and is lightweight for easy portability.
reviewText:
My boyfriend bought this tent at the last minute for a camping trip we took last weekend. We both grew to hate it. It is super easy to set up, it popped open no problem-all we had to do was stake it down. My biggest complaint is the DOOR! The material is pulled so tight that the zipper is nearly impossible to open and close. I am surprised neither of us ripped it out of frustration. Also we got rained on the first night. The tent gets an A+ for no leaks, but every time we opened the door the water would just fall down into our tent---and since the tent was so small on the inside, of course the water went right into our bed. Also a side note about the size of the tent---There is no way you could fit 4 people in there. It barely had enough room for the 2 of us and neither of us are very big.

Folding it down looked confusing, but he seemed to do it pretty easily. I've heard rumors that the springs tend to break after more than 1 use. If we decide to use this tent again I will have to update my review.

title: Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case
description: On-the-go cooking just got a little easier with the Coleman Portable Butane Stove with Carrying Case. With an ultra-portable design and included carry case, this stove is ready for any camping adventure and quickly sets up on any outdoor tabletop, supporting up to a 10-inch pan. Attach an 8.8 oz. butane cylinder (sold separately), light the automatic matchless ignition with the turn of a knob, and in no time, youre cooking with up to 7,650 BTUs of power for up to 1.25 hours. Wind baffles shield your flame from the wind, and a large stable base makes stirring easy. Use your stove for years to come thanks to an easy-to-clean, durable porcelain-coated grate, rust-proof aluminum burner, and made-to-last enameled steel exterior.

title: Coleman Instant Cabin
reviewText:
Great tent well worth it it does not need a rain fly we got caught in a heavy storm and it did not leak at all.  Plenty of room  although I would say it is more like comfy for three people instead of eight. you can pack eight people in but there is only room to walk in and out.  now if you have a pack of cub scouts eight would be no problem.  The tent gets five stars the packaging only three. Amazon has failed again on quality control when shipping items.  This is the reason I gave it three stars.

Setting up was a breeze I do recommend getting heavy duty metal stakes if you are camping in any wind or in the open.  (see image)  These are the style I got for my tent.

description: Coleman Octagon 98 with Full Rainfly Features: - Water-resistant polyester rainfly included with taped seams - provides excellent rain protection - Full length zip-on fly for great ventilation - Exclusive weathered system - Color coded pole attachments for easy set up - Windows and mesh roof - provides maximum ventilation - Removable privacy wall to create separate rooms - Interior storage pockets with cup holders - Room divider with multiple possible configurations Specifications: - Doors: One hinged, one d-door - Rainfly: Polyester taffeta 68d - Inner Tent: Polyester taffeta 68d, polyester mesh 68d - Floor: Polyethylene 1000d - Poles: Steel - Center Height: 6'10" - Base Dimensions: 13' x 13'.
reviewText: We got this tent for Christmas knowing we were going to be going on a National Parks Roadtrip and knowing that we needed a new tent. It has been amazing. Very roomy it goes up quickly and takes down easy. We have never had a problem getting into the bag like with some other tents. The rain fly was missing one of its hooks but it isn't a problem we just hook it differently. AGAIN we love this tent!
text:
TITLE:
Coleman Octagon 98-2 Room Tent

DESCRIPTION:
Coleman Octagon 98 with Full Rainfly Features: - Water-resistant polyester rainfly included with taped seams - provides excellent rain protection - Full length zip-on fly for great ventilation - Exclusive weathered system - Color coded pole attachments for easy set up - Windows and mesh roof - provides maximum ventilation - Removable privacy wall to create separate rooms - Interior storage pockets with cup holders - Room divider with multiple possible configurations Specifications: - Doors: One hinged, one d-door - Rainfly: Polyester taffeta 68d - Inner Tent: Polyester taffeta 68d, polyester mesh 68d - Floor: Polyethylene 1000d - Poles: Steel - Center Height: 6'10" - Base Dimensions: 13' x 13'.

REVIEW:
We got this tent for Christmas knowing we were going to be going on a National Parks Roadtrip and knowing that we needed a new tent. It has been amazing. Very roomy it goes up quickly and takes down easy. We have never had a problem getting into the bag like with some other tents. The rain fly was missing one of its hooks but it isn't a problem we just hook it differently. AGAIN we love this tent!

Full length zip-on fly for great ventilation

Windows and mesh roof - provides maximum ventilation
Removable privacy wall to create separate rooms
Interior storage pockets with cup holders

title: Coleman 40-Can Collapsible Soft Cooler
description: Have the convenience of a large cooler when you need one and extra storage room in your home when you don't. The Coleman 40 Can Collapsible Cooler is big enough to hold almost two cases of soda and then collapse down to the size of a phone book when you're ready to put it away. A bungee cord on the back, front pocket and external lid storage make keeping snacks and utensils convenient. An adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses. Heat-welded seams help prevent soft liner leaks.
reviewText: Great lunch box for work. My only complaint is that the rectangle board that you push to the bottom to give it it's shape is absorbent. Anything that spills in the bag that piece absorbed and after a few weeks had to toss it. I still have the bag though, it's just not always box shaped.

title: Coleman C003 Soft Backpack Cooler
description: Keep your food and snacks cool from the park to the campsite with a Coleman Backpack Cooler. This stylish bag is large enough to hold 28 cans. Extra padding in the back and waist make it even easier to carry over almost any distance. The BPA- and phthalate-free, heat-welded main compartment is perfect for using with ice and the zippered front pocket lets you store extra snacks and small gear. The bungee cord on the front also provides quick access to other items. Antimicrobial, product-protecting properties are built into the flexible lining to resist odor, mold and mildew, making it easy to clean between uses.
reviewText:
It is well made.
I use backpacks like this to bring groceries home on my bike.
I wish someone would make a bigger one, but this is the best one I have found that is a cooler.

title: Coleman Coastal Xtreme Series Marine Portable Cooler, 120 Quart
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for the full length of your next boating excursion with the Coleman 120-Quart Coastal Xtreme 6-Day Marine Cooler. Specially designed for boating and fishing, this portable cooler combines a generous capacity with a rugged design that stands up to tough saltwater conditions. Large enough to store up to 204 cans, the cooler offers plenty of room for beverages, food, and any catch you might haul on board. The insulated lid and walls offer up to 6 days of ice retention, helping ensure your catch stays fresh even during multiday voyages. Built to withstand years of rugged marine use, this heavy-duty cooler comes equipped with rust-resistant stainless steel hardware and UV Guard sun protection that resists yellowing and cracking. A threaded drain with an easy-to-clean valve allows you to empty excess water without tilting the cooler. The smooth EZ-Clean lid easily wipes clean of grime and dirt, while the Have-A-Seat lid offers a comfortable place to sit and relax. And thanks to its comfortable no-crush handle grips, this fishing cooler is easy to transport, whether you're bringing whole meals on a boating trip or heading to your favorite fishing spot.
reviewText: Huge and sturdy.

title: Coleman Xtreme 4 Marine Wheeled Cooler, 40 Quart
description: Take the new, stylish performance of a Coleman 40 Quart Xtreme 4 Marine Wheeled Cooler with you on the boat. While your friends revel in the sleek new look, you'll enjoy the added improvements that better fit your lifestyle. The redesigned 40-quart cooler is tall enough to hold 2-liter bottles upright and large enough to hold up to 60 cans. Four molded cup holders help prevent spills, and the Have-A-Seat Lid supports up to 250 lbs. (113.4 kg) so you'll always have a place to sit and rest. Heavy-duty wheels offer all-terrain transports and a long telescoping handle makes pulling the cooler more comfortable. Inside the cooler's walls, extra Thermo ZONE Insulation and Xtreme Technology holds the cold for four days in temperatures up to 90 F (32.22 C), and still performs amazingly on hotter days. The foam is also free from CFCs, HFCs and HCFCs, which deplete the ozone, and the outside is treated with a UV color additive that resists yellowing and weakening in the case. When the fun is done, the retextured EZ-Clean top makes washing your cooler on wheels simple.
reviewText:
Awsome cooler, works well for pier fishing on 900ft pier near my home.  Managed to stuff 31" Snook in it,
The handle is designed for pulling, like a luggage cart, after dozens of trips and many miles it still holds up.

title: Guide Series Dual-Fuel Camping Stove
description: With fuel-efficient burners and a wind-blocking design, the Coleman Guide Series Dual-Fuel Stove gives you the ability to make family meals for any outdoor adventure. The two independently adjustable Band-a-Blu burners in this portable stove combine for 14,000 BTU of reliable cooking power. Dual Fuel technology conveniently uses either Coleman Liquid Fuel or unleaded gasoline (both sold separately) to efficiently create steady heat with less fuel. This propane stove has adjustable Wind Block panels that provide wind protection while giving you the stovetop space to use two 10-inch pans at the same time. All Season Strong technology helps this propane gas stove function well in all conditions, from summer tailgates to winter hunting trips. The durable chrome-plated grate on this camping stove is removable for easy cleaning.
reviewText: We have one and bought this as a gift for special friends at Christmas.  It was a big hit!  Can't go wrong.

title: Coleman Steel Creek Fast Pitch Dome Tent with Screen Room, 6-Person
description: Enjoy the great outdoors in total comfort with the Coleman Steel Creek Fast Pitch 6-Person Dome Tent. This spacious camping tent features a separate screen room, which offers a comfortable place to lounge while sealing out bugs, debris, and other nuisances. With its full floor, the screen room also provides extra sleeping space on warmer nights. Thanks to Fast Pitch system and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this fast pitch tent assembles 50 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system helps seal out water to keep you and your gear dry, while the included rainfly offers extra protection against rugged weather conditions. With its spacious interior, this dome tent offers enough room for two queen-sized air beds. Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you won't accidentally trip in the dark. And when it's finally time to pack up, the 6-person tent fits neatly inside the expandable carry bag for easy storage and transportation. A range of other convenient amenities--including storage pockets, window awnings, and an E-port for routing electrical power--round out this fully-featured camping tent.
reviewText: Front zipper as you raise it goes away from you so you need to lean over to get fully zipped up.

title: Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup
description: Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.

And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.

The rainfly leaked in the seam though the shower was not heavy. I will probably return it.

Coleman 8-Person Camping Tent with Built-in Closet | Tenaya Lake Cabin Tent with Fast Pitch Setup

DESCRIPTION:
Enjoy some comforts from home while camping with the Coleman Tenaya Lake Fast Pitch 8-Person Cabin Tent with Closet. This camping tent includes a built-in closet with shelves and a hanger bar, making it easy to keep your clothes and gear organized without having to dig through your backpack. Thanks to pre-attached, color-coded top poles and hub, fast-fit feet, and snag-free Insta-Clip suspension, this cabin tent with the Fast Pitch system assembles 55 percent quicker than similar Coleman tents with a conventional setup. The WeatherTec system with patented welded floors and inverted seams helps ensure you and your gear stay dry, while the included rainfly offers extra weather protection. This backpacking tent offers enough room for two queen air beds and comes with a room divider for added privacy and storage pockets to keep your items stored away. An E-Port lets you easily bring power inside the tent. A hinged door lets you enter and exit the tent effortlessly, and Illumiline reflective guy lines offer greater visibility at night, so you wont accidentally trip in the dark. And when its time to pack up, the 8-person tent fits neatly inside the included wheeled carry bag for easy storage and transportation.

title: Coleman Micro Packer LED Lantern, 100 L
description: Grab a lantern and a flashlight in the palm of your hand with the Coleman MicroPacker LED Lantern. The bright, 2-in-1 design snaps from area lighting to directional lighting quite literally in a snap. The bright LED lasts a lifetime, so it never needs to be replaced. The versatile light lasts up to 92 hours, and an extremely lightweight design is only 6 oz.-even with the three AA-cell batteries that power the lantern.
reviewText:
DOES NOT convert to a flashlight. No way, no how. Just like the old Coleman gasoline lanterns had a metal reflector that could shield one side? This has an inch-wide black (!) plastic barrier that slides up, so it doesn't shine back in your eyes. That doesn't make it into a "flashlight" in any way. It doesn't focus or concentrate the light in any way. It just blocks a narrow strip from your eyes.

Other than that, it looks decently made, and the 3xAA-or-1xD battery feature is nice. But I was expecting something like the Eveready lights, that pop up to be a lantern, and pop down to be a focused flashlight. This isn't/

title: Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+
description: The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ offers both a grill and a stove for versatile campsite cooking. This durable steel-crafted outdoor appliance has a stove surface that fits a 10-inch pan as well as a 130-square inch grill area, letting you saut veggies while you're grilling chicken, or get the soup going while you're cooking up burgers. Each surface has independent temperature controls to give you precise control. The InstaStart push-button ignition powers up the portable grill with a simple press of a button with no matches needed, while integrated Wind Block panels help shield the flame from wind and can be folded down on calm days to be used as side tables. This outdoor grill features PerfectFlow pressure control technology to keep the heat steady even in extreme conditions and PerfectHeat technology for more efficient cooking with less fuel. An aluminized steel cooktop offers durable use and is rust resistant, and the removable grates and grease management tray offer simple and quick cleanup. The Coleman Camp Propane Grill/Stove+ can run for up to one hour with both burners on high setting on one 16.4-ounce propane cylinder (sold separately) and produces 20,000 total BTUs of cooking power.
reviewText: I had high expectations for this grill. Yes, it did heat up things fast. The drawback is that the legs are only an inch tall. Which means if the table you are using is not a heat resistant surface,it will ruin your table. Second, there is no high cover you can purchase, if you want to grill something like,corn, or other meat that you want to cook longer, you cannot. Because the cover is about an inch high only. I just wish there is a cover I can buy for the grill side.

title: Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler, Tan
description: Food, drinksand the funlast longer with the Coleman 70-Quart Xtreme 5-Day Heavy-Duty Cooler. To be precise, this cooler keeps ice up to 5 days at temps as high as 90F thanks to an insulated lid and extra insulation in the walls. Large enough to hold 100 cans, this spacious cooler transports and chills enough food and drinks to keep the entire party fueled and refreshed for the duration of your adventures. The comfort-grip, no-crush handles make it easier to lift your cooler in and out of the car or onto a picnic bench with no pinching. Once you arrive, the Have-A-Seat lid ensures you have a place to sit and relax, and the cup holders molded into the lid keep your drinks close and prevent spills. When the fun is done, the EZ-Clean top has a smoother surface that wipes clean easily, and the leak-resistant channel drain lets you empty any excess water without tilting the cooler. You'll be ready for your next fun adventure in no time.
reviewText:
This is a very good basic cooler. We're using it on a road trip and it works well. I used a couple frozen gallon jugs of water as ice and prechilled both the cooler itself as well as our drinks. Three days into the trip and everything is still nice and cold with the ice about half melted.

I especially like the size, which is compact for how much it holds. The recessed handles are nice since they don't catch on the other luggage in the back of the van. Just an all around good cooler.

title: Coleman Trailhead II Cot
reviewText:
Well - I should have believed all the comments about installing the crossbar!  The wife and I spent 30 minutes trying everything to get the 2nd crossbar in place on both cots - no go.  It is simply impossible unless you use a screwdriver or some type of wedge.  I REFUSE to use a screwdriver to put together a darn cot!  Hey, if Amazon wants to cover return postage for everyone with this problem - I guess it is in their cost model.  I for one will never buy another coleman product.  Obviously they know the problem but simply don't care.

UPDATE:  Just sent Coleman a e-mail outlining my problem (and that of others) - will let ya know what they say.
FLASH - REPLY:  HOLD ONTO YOU SEATS MY FRIENDS!  Coleman replied and said they NEVER HEARD of the crossbar problem.  I believe that says it all - unreal.

reviewText: When I got them and opened them up 1 was already damaged. This isn't a review about the seller as they are willing to help by exchanging however I need them and I need them now. Been burnt on everything I've bought of there's for years and I'm done with them. I would recommend spending a little extra. It'll go a long way in the end. These are not military grade. Come to think of it pretty sure the US Military doesn't use anything made by Coleman. I could be wrong tho.

title: Coleman Instant Dome 5 Person Tent with Integrated Rainfly
description: The Coleman 5-Person Instant Cabin tent makes camping easy so you can enjoy every moment of your outdoor adventure. In about a minute you can have the tent set up or taken down. The spacious tent fits two queen-sized airbeds. The exclusive WeatherTec system, and a well integrated rainfly keep you safer from the weather. You'll love the Illumiline reflective guy lines, as well as the pre-attached poles of this instant cabin. Nothing kills a campsite buzz like a tent with too many poles and too little instruction. Enter the Coleman Instant Dome Tent , a five-person tent that sets up in less than a minute. The tent is made using an innovative one-piece design, with poles that come pre-attached to the tent. That means there's no frustrating assembly and no parts to lose. Simply unfold the tent, spread out the fabric, extend the poles, and click and lock. The only thing easier is rolling out your bag and sleeping in the open. The tent is outfitted with Coleman's exclusive WeatherTec system, a series of technical features intended to protect you from inclement weather and nasty storms. WeatherTec tents are built with a coated polyester fabric combined with anti-wicking thread, webbing, and zippers, all designed to keep you dry when the rain comes down.
reviewText:
I really wanted to love this tent.  It was just what I was looking for, and much lighter in weight than the instant tents from before.  I read all the reviews that I could find, and though they were mixed, I decided that for the price, I didn't expect it to last forever so I jumped in.  Based on pricing, I opted to buy online at Costco.

One of the bridge poles was broken and there were no tent stakes,  and it was most definitely a new tent that I purchased.

I am so glad that I attempted to set up the tent prior to taking it out camping.  It could have been a bad start to a good time.  I am returning it.

Since I didn't get to use it, I can't report to how easy it was to put up, or it's features.

The instructions provided are minimal so if you decide to go for it, watch the video on YouTube.

title: Coleman Kids Quad Chair
description: Have a cold drink at hand at the campsite, BBQ or picnic with a Coleman Cooler Quad Chair. The cooler built into the armrest holds up to 4 cans and provides easy access to a cold drink once the one in your mesh cup holder is empty. While you sip on your favorite beverage, the fully cushioned seat and back only adds to your relaxation. If you want your hands free, the side pockets work nicely to store your book, magazine or some snacks. When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.
reviewText:
These chairs are adorable and seem to be well made.  I have to say though that they seem most appropriate for kids aged 4 to 7.

Buyer beware!!  I tried to return two of these chairs and was offered a total refund of $8.66.  What a rip-off.

When the fun is done, the chair easily folds up into a carry bag for easy transport and storage.

REVIEW:
These chairs are adorable and seem to be well made.  I have to say though that they seem most appropriate for kids aged 4 to 7.

Buyer beware!!  I tried to return two of these chairs and was offered a total refund of $8.66.  What a rip-off.

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText: Horrible Tent.  I'm just glad I only had to pay $60 for this piece of junk.  This tent lasted 7 days from light use during RAGBRAI 2017.  The overall design of the tent is awful.  The setup is pretty easy, but trying to repack the tent and figure out how to fold it back together is a nightmare.  There are a few pictures to show the setup, but nothing on how to repack the tent.  Not even on youtube.  If you make one wrong fold you'll break a tent pole rolling the tent back up.  The poles on my tent got several stress fractures resulting in one breaking in half (not from setup or repacking). Once the tent is setup it is nice and sturdy and plenty of room for a cot or two.  Just the overall design of the tent leaves a little to be desired.  Just to much going on.  The other bad part of this tent is the damn zippers.  I understand that zippers will get caught in the fabric every now an then, but this thing is constantly getting the zipper caught in the green flap around the door. A serious pain in the a$$. I filed a warranty with Coleman, so we'll see how good their customer service is for this fiasco. Knocking of two stars for the defective tent poles and one star for the zippers

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText: Sleeping in the rain. Bought this for hunting trips. First trip,  tent leaks at all the seams,  zippers are cheap, get caught in rain guard,  but it is a disaster as far as keeping you dry. I have a puddle of water under my mat, my sleeping bags are wet ,  clothes are wet and I'm forced to cut short hunting trip as it's cold, and all my clothes, sleeping bags are wet. Absolutely worthless. To be fair, it has rained every night , hard,  however, that's why you have a tent. The zippers are,cheap,  get tangled in the rain guard, the vents work well, unfortunately if you want to close them you can't. So,cold,air is a companion if you are,camping in the cold. This is a two man tent. Enough room for two single air mattresses. Of course you would be touching the sides, and then both. Would get wet. The hanger for the lantern works, just make sure you use a small lantern as it is only 4 feet tall. I read reviews saying you can stand in tent. Yes,  frodo bagins and pippin would have no problem. Unfortunately human beings of normal,height do. I have been a fan of Coleman all my life,  I still have a 20 year old stove, I'm sleeping in wet Coleman sleeping bags , this is the worst excuse for a tent in my life. Coleman should be ashamed.

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText:
Just an absolute disaster.  First off, the directions (which are basically non-existent) make it look SO easy; which it would be if Coleman had installed the brackets and pieces right. But they didn't.  We have been fighting with this stupid tent for over 3 hours and finally gave up in spite of reading the reviews of what is wrong with it (basically, brackets are upside down, our circular piece was even upside down, and a pole that is supposed to extend doesn't extend on the top part... very frustrating.

Secondly, you need to stake all four corners VERY securely in order for it to stay up.  As everyone knows, this doesn't always work that great in some soils (I'm thinking Arches National Park.)  It's part of the design that it's necessary though, especially since the middle doesn't have the proper tension to it to keep it up on it's own.

I am VERY FRUSTRATED that I am going to end up spending about 75.00 in taxes (yes, Viners now have to pay taxes on their items they receive to review, so they are not really free anymore)  for a completely useless dud and lemon.  If only I could return the tent I would in a heartbeat to get my money back.

Even IF it worked right, The tent is nothing fancy and the price tag is a bit high for the size and what you get.  I've gotten cheaper at Sam's Club for a tent twice the size that only takes 20 minutes to install.  NOT worth it.

title: Coleman Camping Instant Signal Mountain Tent
reviewText:
When I first opened this and laid it out, the directions made no sense whatsoever. Fortunately I was able to figure it out and get the tent set up in a snap... or so I thought. Then I noticed the roof of the tent was sagging. Although all of the side poles had snapped easily into place, I hadn't been able to get the top one to snap at all. After multiple additional attempts and enlisting ever-stronger people to help me (ending with a guy who's 6'5"), we FINALLY got it to snap into place. When we finally got back home and set it up to air out, the same thing happened--we needed two strong adults playing tug-of-war on the top beam to get it locked. If I had purchased it rather than getting it free for review, I would have returned it.  As it is, I will never take it camping unless I have another adult with me, and/or a second tent as backup.

In case mine was simply defective, here are some other thoughts:
* Lots tent for your money - it's very tall inside and would fit 8 sleeping mats laid out flat on the floor, so it's exceptionally roomy for 3-6 people
* Weatherproofing is OK but not great - we had a couple hours of light rain and although we had a handful of puddles in the tent they were small--a tablespoon here and there rather than drenched. We were at a beach with 90-degree days and 75-degree nights so this wasn't a problem for us, but it would be a concern in more rain, or if camping in colder weather
* Includes a divider to separate the tent into two halves, which is great for privacy while changing. I was too short (5'2") to attach the one at the top of the tent, as I simply couldn't reach that high. But we did okay leaving that one loose / it turned the divider into something more like a stall in a dressing room-- provided ventilation, but you couldn't see over it
* The zippers are mildly annoying. They would snag two to three times every single time we opened or closed the zipper over the entire week of camping **but** it was super easy to unsnag, so this was a mild annoyance rather than a big one
* The tent includes two pockets for hanging small items like car keys or a phone. I had expected zero so this was a welcome surprise, although my other tents have more pockets, and more would have been helpful here too
* Six windows provide good ventilation. They don't unzip all the way--I assume for weather proofing--but they unzip enough to get the job done
* Weighs 26 pounds so it's lighter than most toddlers and easy for one person to carry, you just wouldn't want to use it for backpacking; this is for car camping only
* The tall size means it's a good source of shade when camping in overly sunny areas

Overall, it's a decent tent once set up, but getting the roof to stay put is fiendishly difficult, and more so for a tent that purports to be easy.

title: Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern
description: The Coleman Divide Plus Spin 525L LED Lantern features a unique twisting design that lets you rotate the entire lantern to easily change modes.Impact-resistant up to 1 m.
reviewText: For sturdy, this in no way competes with the Coleman lanterns of my youth--my earliest memory in the mid-1950s ran on a can of fuel and lit the night when needed in a farm setting, a real tool. The sturdiest part is the metal D-ring handle on the bottom that one uses to engage the "use batteries" or "saves batteries" feature. It is all plastic, and not very impressive plastic, at that--the 4 D batteries are as heavy as the entire housing.  However, this lightness contributes to its ability to purportedly survive a drop on a hard surface up to one meter. Overall, the size is similar to a two liter bottle, minus the screw top. The best thing going for it is the ability to protect the battery life, when not in use and the choice of light output to make the burning lamp last as long as possible. One will have to decide if the investment for one emergency light source is worth the going price on the day one shops.

title: Coleman Crescent Mummy Bag
description:
Coleman design and high-tech insulation combine for maximized warmth and superior comfort. The Coleman Crescent Mummy Bag boasts 48 ounces of hollow-core Coletherm insulation, to trap air for warmth and keep you comfortable all the way down to 15 F. Measuring 32"x82", the Crescent bag fits most people up to 6'2". The Diamond ripstop polyester cover is rugged and tear-resistant, with a comfortable polyester liner. Stuff sack storage. The Coleman 8185B558 Crescent 15-degree Tall Sleeping Bag is a comfortable four-season, mummy-style sleeping bag. At 32-inches by 82-inches, the tall-size Crescent fits most body types up to six-feet-two-inches long. With 38 ounces of Hollofil-808 insulation, this extreme-temperature sleeping bag keeps you warm in temperatures down to 15-degrees Fahrenheit. Features include a nylon cover with a nylon liner and a stuff sack. All Coleman sleeping bags are designed with ComfortSmart technology to keep you comfortable. The Crescent's ComfortSmart features include: ZipPlow which plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags, Fiberlock which prevents insulation from shifting and increases durability, Thermlock which reduces heat loss through the zipper thereby keeping you warmer, Zipper Glide tailoring which allows smooth zipper operation around the corner, and a Certified Temperature Rating that is independently tested for temperature accuracy. This Coleman sleeping bag is designed to provide the most comfortable overnight stay, at home and away. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty Amazon.com Sleeping Bag Guide
Sleep Well: Finding the Right Sleeping Bag
Sleeping bag technology has come a long way from the days of cowboy bedrolls. These days, there are a number of high-tech materials and designs available to keep you warm during the coldest outings. Here's a short list of things to keep in mind when you're shopping for a bag: Buy for Cold
It's a safe bet that on at least one of your adventures, the nighttime temperature will drop unexpectedly. That's why it's smart to buy a bag that's rated for the lowest possible temperature you expect to face on your camping and backpacking trips. For summer trips, a bag rated at +35 degrees or higher will likely do the trick. If you like to camp in higher elevations in the summer, or if spring and fall outings are in your future, consider bags rated from +10 to +35. Winter adventurers should look for bags in the -10 to +10 range, while those on serious winter alpine climbs and expeditions will want a bag rated lower than -10. Keep in mind that sleeping bag manufacturers' temperature ratings only estimate the minimum temperature at which the bag will provide warmth. Take these numbers with a grain of salt, as different folks generate different amounts of heat when they sleep. If you're the type who likes to pile on the covers even on warmer nights, go for a bag that's rated ten degrees colder. The opposite is true for "warm" sleepers--a 35-degree bag will probably work for you on a 25-degree night. Goose or No Goose?
The most important component of any sleeping bag is its insulating material. Modern sleeping bags offer two choices: goose down or synthetic. While both materials have advantages and disadvantages, down bags are considered superior because of their phenomenal warmth-to-weight and warmth-to-bulk ratios. While providing great insulation, down is extremely compressible and light. There's a reason why geese can fly and stay warm through the winter! Down also boasts great long-term durability and will typically retain its insulating properties after years of use. All of that said, there are many high-quality synthetic bags on the market and synthetic materials are getting better all the time. While a synthetic bag will weigh somewhat more than a down bag at an equivalent temperature rating, synthetic bags perform better when wet. (Yes, the Achilles heel of down is that it loses all insulating properties when wet.) If your trips take you to wet climates, you may want to consider a synthetic bag for this reason alone. Keep in mind, too, that many people are allergic to down--synthetic bags are non-allergenic. Finally, down is considerably more expensive than synthetic, which might tip the balance for adventurers on a budget. Bags for All Shapes
Sleeping bags come in two basic shapes that reflect their intended use. Mummy-shaped bags offer the best warmth because they conform to the body's contours. This minimizes the amount of body heat the body must put out to maintain a constant temperature. Many mummy bags are offered in women-specific shapes and sizes, as well. Rectangular bags, while they do offer more room to toss and turn, are less thermally efficient because they contain more open air space. Also, they are typically heavier than mummy bags, and are generally not offered with down insulation, making them best suited for car camping or short backpacking trips. Pad Yourself
No matter what kind of bag you choose, a sleeping pad is a required accessory. Not only do they provide much-needed comfort when sleeping on the ground, pads also offer crucial warmth for your backside, as the weight of your body compresses--and renders virtually useless--the sleeping bag insulation that lies beneath you.
reviewText: This bag was supposed to be 15 degrees and I took it camping in a low of 45 and I was freezing!  I run cold, and am 5'2''; but I thought that if I bought a 15 degree bag it would have at least kept me warm in a temperature in 45 degrees!  I sent this bag back.  Also, the color wasn't what was in the picture; although, that didn't really bother me.

The Coleman 8185B558 Crescent 15-degree Tall Sleeping Bag is a comfortable four-season, mummy-style sleeping bag. At 32-inches by 82-inches, the tall-size Crescent fits most body types up to six-feet-two-inches long. With 38 ounces of Hollofil-808 insulation, this extreme-temperature sleeping bag keeps you warm in temperatures down to 15-degrees Fahrenheit. Features include a nylon cover with a nylon liner and a stuff sack. All Coleman sleeping bags are designed with ComfortSmart technology to keep you comfortable. The Crescent's ComfortSmart features include: ZipPlow which plows fabric away from the zipper to prevent snags, Fiberlock which prevents insulation from shifting and increases durability, Thermlock which reduces heat loss through the zipper thereby keeping you warmer, Zipper Glide tailoring which allows smooth zipper operation around the corner, and a Certified Temperature Rating that is independently tested for temperature accuracy. This Coleman sleeping bag is designed to provide the most comfortable overnight stay, at home and away. Manufacturer Warranty
Five-year warranty Amazon.com Sleeping Bag Guide
Sleep Well: Finding the Right Sleeping Bag
Sleeping bag technology has come a long way from the days of cowboy bedrolls. These days, there are a number of high-tech materials and designs available to keep you warm during the coldest outings. Here's a short list of things to keep in mind when you're shopping for a bag: Buy for Cold
It's a safe bet that on at least one of your adventures, the nighttime temperature will drop unexpectedly. That's why it's smart to buy a bag that's rated for the lowest possible temperature you expect to face on your camping and backpacking trips. For summer trips, a bag rated at +35 degrees or higher will likely do the trick. If you like to camp in higher elevations in the summer, or if spring and fall outings are in your future, consider bags rated from +10 to +35. Winter adventurers should look for bags in the -10 to +10 range, while those on serious winter alpine climbs and expeditions will want a bag rated lower than -10. Keep in mind that sleeping bag manufacturers' temperature ratings only estimate the minimum temperature at which the bag will provide warmth. Take these numbers with a grain of salt, as different folks generate different amounts of heat when they sleep. If you're the type who likes to pile on the covers even on warmer nights, go for a bag that's rated ten degrees colder. The opposite is true for "warm" sleepers--a 35-degree bag will probably work for you on a 25-degree night. Goose or No Goose?

While a synthetic bag will weigh somewhat more than a down bag at an equivalent temperature rating, synthetic bags perform better when wet. (Yes, the Achilles heel of down is that it loses all insulating properties when wet.) If your trips take you to wet climates, you may want to consider a synthetic bag for this reason alone. This minimizes the amount of body heat the body must put out to maintain a constant temperature. Many mummy bags are offered in women-specific shapes and sizes, as well. Rectangular bags, while they do offer more room to toss and turn, are less thermally efficient because they contain more open air space. Also, they are typically heavier than mummy bags, and are generally not offered with down insulation, making them best suited for car camping or short backpacking trips. Pad Yourself

title: Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen
description: The Coleman Exponent Outfitter Camp Kitchen is a complete, simple-to-set-up outdoor food preparation center. A spacious countertop, sink, pantry, and cooking stand are combined in this efficient unit to make preparing meals in the outdoors easy and enjoyable. No assembly is required -- the Outfitter Camp Kitchen simply unfolds and is ready to go to work. Its ideal for camping, backyard barbecues, picnics, beach parties, and tailgating. Measuring over six feet long (72.5") and over four feet high (50") when fully opened, the Camp Kitchen offers a spacious meal-prep area. It features a spacious polished-aluminum work surface for preparing foods, a side extender shelf for holding a camp stove, and a removable ABS plastic sink with drain plug and hose for doing dishes, rinsing vegetables or cleaning the days catch. There are built-in sections for just about everything else, including a paper-towel holder, chrome-plated wire hooks for cups and utensils, and shelving for spices or food storage. Weighing only 35 pounds, the Camp Kitchen folds down to a compact 42" x 21.75" x 7.5" and fits easily in most car trunks. It carries like a suitcase in its own rugged zippered nylon storage case, so its ready to go on a moments notice. The Outfitter Camp Kitchen can be used with legs extended to stand on its own, or with the legs folded underneath and placed on a tabletop or tailgate. Its durable enough to withstand heavy use, with strong, double-reinforced corners, positive stainless steel latches, a heavy-duty aluminum frame with stable leg braces for added support, and zinc-plated storage racks, dipped in PVC for extra rust-resistance. What's more, with the lid closed over the work surface between meals, the Camp Kitchen becomes a handy game table: enjoy playing backgammon, chess or checkers on the molded-in game boards; checker and backgammon pieces are even included! Simplify cooking for large groups on car and RV camping expeditions or tailgating parties with the Coleman Exponent 2142A700 Outfitter camp kitchen--a complete outdoor food preparation center. This two-tiered table system features a main preparation and storage area and a side extender shelf that easily holds most Coleman stoves. The storage/prep area includes a removable sink with drain plug and hose, paper towel holder, and chrome plated wire hooks and shelving for additional storage. It has a closeable lid with painted game boards (checkers/backgammon pieces included). It's made from heavy-duty channel extruded aluminum for added strength and features stable leg braces for added support, a convenient carry handle and rugged storage bag.
reviewText:
If you're thinking of buying this, DON'T. YOU DON'T NEED IT. My friend has one, and it's the most useless thing we've ever taken camping, and WAY more trouble than it's worth. If you have enough camping cups and dishes to fill the hooks and shelves, you have too many. Who wants to spend time washing all that stuff (including the unit itself) with freezing cold water in the woods?? Plus it's wobbly on uneven ground (ALL CAMP SITES) and there's really no reason to have a sink if you don't also have running water.

Anyway, isn't part of the fun of camping learning to simplify and improvise, instead of bringing your house and all the complicated maintenance that goes with it into the wilderness with you?

title: Coleman Exponent Multi-Fuel Stove
description: The Coleman Multi-Fuel Stove offers sturdy, compact design and versatile, powerful performance. The integrated fuel tank operates on your choice of Coleman fuel, unleaded gasoline or kerosene (kerosene generator included). The sturdy fold-out legs keep the stove stable, while burner bowl and pot supports help protect the flame even in windy conditions. Precise flame control allows you to go from simmer to boil. Stove weighs 21.6 ounces. Get ready to cook with the Coleman Exponent 550B725 Multi-Fuel camp stove, which weighs 21.6 ounces. Fill up the integrated tank one time and use it all weekend. It operates on either Coleman fuel or unleaded gasoline, and can also operate on kerosene with an optional generator. Thanks to Coleman's PerfectFlow feature, it's regulated for a constant, consistent burn (without flaring or flickering). Other features include fold-out legs and wind-protecting burner bowel and pot supports. The Coleman Exponent 550-6771 Maintenance Kit is a recommended accessory.
reviewText: I wanted to like this stove while I used it for car camping.  But it has one very serious flaw.  It won't turn off and spills unburned fuel creating a massive fire hazard.  It wouldn't turn off when brand new.  So I called Coleman support who were very helpful.  We determined which part failed and they sent out a replacement, no charge.  I installed it and it ran fine for several years but recently spilled a cup of white gas on the tailgate because, again, it wouldn't turn off.  It is too dangerous to keep so I threw it in the trash.  The off switch should be a needle valve but this design is just a thin wire running through the top tube to plug the jet.  Use this at your own risk

title: Coleman 6-Day 50 Qt. Ultimate Xtreme Wheeled Cooler
description:
With extra insulation and rugged design, the Coleman Ultimate Xtreme Cooler is in it for the long haul. Two inches of insulation in the lid and case provide superior cold retention: it will keep food, beverages and ice for up to 6 days at outside temperatures up to 90 F. This 50-quart cooler can hold up to 72 cans with ice. The cooler's large, sturdy wheels are designed for pulling the cooler through rough terrain. A comfortable tow handle provides one-handed portability, while two-way carry handles make it easy to lift or move. The convenient hinged lid sports four built-in drink holders, each a reliable 2" deep. The lid is designed to accommodate 24" elastic cords (sold separately) for additional storage. A rustproof, leak-resistant Channel Drain provides easy, no-tilt draining of melted ice or other liquids. Color: ice green. Take your Coleman 50-Quart Wheeled Ultimate Cooler on the road, to the job site, on vacation, or to game day. With plenty of food and beverage storage space, four stay-put beverage holders, all-terrain wheels, and an easy-grasp tow handle, this ice green cooler can handle any event. The chest cooler stores ice for up to six days at temperatures of up to 90 degrees and can hold 72 beverage cans with ice. Made of durable plastic, the exterior resists scratching and denting, and the interior features a Channel Drain for easy, no-tilt draining and clean-up. This models lid can be fitted with 24-inch cords (not included) for additional dry storage--lawn chairs, picnic blankets, beach towels--on top. About Coleman
The Coleman Company has been creating and innovating products for recreational outdoor use since W.C. Coleman started selling gasoline-powered lanterns in 1900. Inventor of the hugely popular fold-up camp stove, Coleman developed a plastic liner for his galvanized steel coolers in 1957--the birth of the modern cooler--and the company has been improving their utility and design ever since. The array of products that bear the Coleman name now includes just about everything you might need to work or play outdoors, from tents and sleeping bags to boats, backpacks, and furniture.
reviewText: doesn't have that great of a seal.  can turn it upside down and the lid will flip open without any hesitation.  highly disappointed in the product.  I placed a flashlight inside the box and was able to see a lot of light coming thru.  not sure how this will keep anything cold more than a day or two at the most.  I'm blessed that there is an easy return policy from the seller.  I wouldn't recommend this series of Coleman coolers.
