title: Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Thermoelectric Cooler
description: You choose: keep your food warm OR cool, and never need ice or a freezer pack, with the Coleman 40-Quart PowerChill Hot/Cold Cooler. The PowerChill uses electricity to keep your food at the right temperature. On Cool mode, it cools down to forty degrees below the surrounding temperature; on Warm, it heats to 110-140 degrees above the surrounding temperature. Just plug it into your car's 12-volt outlet, or purchase the 110-volt AC adapter (sold separately) for use in the home. The PowerChill 40 Quart can hold 44 cans, plus snacks. The interior tray helps keep everything organized, and you can use the PowerChill upright, cabinet-style, or horizontally, chest-style. The door can be converted to open from the left or the right, so it'll work well in your available space. 1-year limited warranty.
reviewText:
It actually just came in today. I have it connected to a 10 amp power supply that came with a 12v receptacle. So far it's dropping its internal temp 10 degrees per hour.
Note 1: It arrived in perfect shape, and appears to be very well built and finished. I particularly like this line in the Manual:
  "This cooler will not chill foods and beverages as fast as your home refrigerator. Please don't expect it to do so."
Note 2:  It does NOT come with an AC adapter of any kind, contrary to some of the reviews on Amazon. However, the Manual tells how to order
  one.
Note 3:  I had an earlier version of this cooler for 5 or 10 years, and might have killed it by putting ice and icy foods in it.

title: Coleman 42-Can Soft Cooler with Removable Liner & Wheels
description: The Coleman 42-Can Wheeled Soft Cooler goes with you to the campsite, the BBQ, sporting events, tailgates and beyond. It features durable wheels and a retractable handle, so it's easy and comfortable to transport to wherever the party is. The cooler is big enough to hold 42 cans, and features plenty of extra storage space in its front zippered pocket, side mesh pockets, and mesh pocket in the lid. There are even bungees on the lid for stowing more extras! The cooler is soft on the outside and has a hard plastic liner inside to protect the contents from being crushed. The liner material is FDA approved for food contact, and is removable for easy cleaning. The Soft Cooler provides to resist odors, mold, and mildew.
reviewText:
This cooler really works quite well. I bought it based on good reviews and the fact that it claimed to hold 42 standard 12oz. beverage cans. C'mon, let's be real, that means beers. Anyway, with the plastic insert installed, I can't find any way to fit more than 36 cans in this. Not a deal-breaker, but I'm not sure where the extra 6 cans are supposed to go.

The plastic liner, after just a few weeks of ownership, has already saved me a ton of hassle when cleaning up after a picnic or rooftop drinking session with friends. We've had leaky cans, not-quite-empty bottles, and trash in this cooler so far and the liner is super easy to remove and rinse off with the hose. Dealing with a dirty and wet interior of the cooler would be quite a bit more trouble. Great job, Coleman!

I appreciate the small, but useful, exterior pockets, including one that zips closed. Good for your wallet, keys, sunglasses, phone, and maybe a small bluetooth speaker. The handle is a good length for an average-height adult and the wheels, while small, seem to handle rolling through the grass just fine. A big downside to the handle is that it doesn't lock, so picking the cooler up one-handed isn't easy and you also can't lean on the handle when it's extended. This would be my one suggestion for improvement.

title: Coleman 9 x 7 ft. Instant Canopy
description: Quickly create shade for sporting events, tailgates, and other outdoor occasions with the Coleman Instant Beach Canopy. This pop up canopy assembles in three minutes in just three steps to create a shady area outdoors. The canopy cover is made with 150 D UVGuard material that offers 50+ UPF protection from the suns harmful rays. The lightweight pre-attached telescoping poles can be assembled without having to reach over the head, so anyone can set up the tent easily, while comfort grips lock the shelter into place with pinch-free adjustability. Once the canopy tent is assembled, it offers stable shelter on grass, concrete, and asphalt thanks to its welded steel feet. And when its time to pack up, the Coleman Instant Beach Canopy can be stored and transported in the included easy-pull wheeled carry bag, which fits inside most cars.

title: Coleman Instant Dome Tent
reviewText:
Good quality tent overall. But...
1. the main selling point; its quick set up; is exaggerated. You can save a minute or two by not fumbling around with tent poles. However you still have to hammer in stakes in the 4 corners as well as the guy lines. Very little time saved there. Also, the tent must be folded up I a very specific way to fit back in the bag. Took me a few tries to figure out the fold up order.
2. If the permanently attached poles break, you may be stuck with a tent that cant be pitched.
3. The built in rain fly is a pain. It is too easily caught in the door zipper. And offers no additional material extending outward from the door like some models.
4. Coleman claims this is a 3 person tent. As usual, that notion is laughable. Yes, you can squeeze 3 adults in. But only if everyone sleeps in a full straightened out position with no movement at all.
5. No bargain on price considering the above limitations. I would have had the same camping experience with my traditional Coleman 3 person tent at about $40 less.

reviewText: Having been in Cub Scouts and Boy Scouts in my younger years, I had grown fond of these simple yet very dependable mess kits while on various outings. They are made of aluminum to save weight in your gear pack and hold up well to extended uses for trips last two to three weeks or longer. Easy to clean up and take up very little room in your gear pack. Saving space in your pack, as any outdoors person knows is a very welcomed advantage. I, for one, am really glad to see these mess kits still on the market.

title: Coleman 24-Hour 16-Can Cooler
description: Keep your food and drinks cold for an extended time when you bring a Coleman 24-Hour 16-Can Cooler to the park or the campsite. Extra insulation keeps your items cold for up to 24 hours. The zippered main compartment also helps seal in the cold, while a front pocket lets you store extra snacks. No matter where you go or what extras you bring, an adjustable shoulder strap makes it easy to carry. Once you're back home, the antimicrobial liner resists odor, mold, mildew and fungus on the product, making it easy to clean between each adventure.
reviewText: I had high hopes for this cooler, my expectations were not met. I used this for 2 days at work (12hr shifts indoors 75F). I tested this with 2 different sets of ice packs. I used mostly pre-chilled foods and drinks with just a couple things like pretzels being not pre-chilled and both lunches were very similar in amounts and items. The first day was with a Arctic Ice Alaskan Series Reusable Cooler Pack, 2.5-Pound, the second day was with 4 of the Set of 5 Cooler Shock lunch bag size ice packs - high performance 18 degree Fahrenheit using phase change science to achieve 8-10 hour cooling - avoid spoilage so you can eat your lunch!. The cooler failed to get down to fridge temps(below 40F), and failed to maintain temps with both sets of cooler packs. I took temperatures with a Elitech RC4 Temperature Data Logger Recorder with External Temperature Sensor every 5 min both days. I attached a pic of the graph. Over all I cannot recommend this cooler, it does not have enough insulation.
